actions.thought: K9o from UTG is too weak to open in 6-max. It's easily dominated by better kings and plays poorly out of position. Standard fold.

Hand is 9♠3♦ offsuit — very weak, unconnected, and poor to play out of position. There are players left to act behind me (cutoff, button, and the blinds), so calling 100 or risking a raise would put me in difficult multiway postflop spots with a hand that rarely has fold equity or good equity. With 100bb effective stacks and many better spots to pick later, the correct move is to conserve chips and fold preflop.

I am in the Cutoff with 3s 6d, which is a very weak starting hand. While two players have folded, there are still three players left to act behind me. Opening with 63 offsuit from this position is not profitable in the long run. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Folded to me on the button with ATo offsuit, a strong hand in this position with 6-handed play implied. Standard to open raise for value and position. Early in tournament with deep stacks, use a small sizing of 2.5x the BB (250 total) to build pot while keeping ranges wide and denying equity to blinds.

I hold Tc 2h in the Small Blind, which is a statistically very weak hand (T-2 offsuit). Facing a raise to 250 from the Button, I would need to call 200 more into a pot that would be 600. While the raw pot odds (33%) are close to my raw equity against a wide button range, my equity realization out of position with such a disconnected hand is extremely poor. It is not profitable to defend this hand. The standard play is to fold.

Facing a 2.5x button open out of position with 9♠5♦ offsuit, this hand has poor playability and is frequently dominated when we make a pair. With no blockers and no compelling reason to 3-bet bluff, the marginal pot odds don’t overcome the positional disadvantage and reverse implied odds. Preserve stack and fold.

I’m UTG with 2♠3♣ — a very weak, offsuit, low-value hand with minimal straight/flush potential and almost no showdown value. In early position at a 6-handed table I should be playing a tight opening range. Limping (calling 100) hands like this just gives better-positioned opponents cheap odds and leads to difficult multiway, out-of-position decisions; raising is also wrong — this hand has negative expected value as an open. With 100bb stacks and a long tournament ahead, preserve chips and avoid marginal spots. Fold.

Q3o is a very weak hand to play from middle position in a 6-max game. It lacks connectivity and has a poor kicker, making it unprofitable to open or call with. I will fold and wait for a better starting hand.

Unopened pot after two folds (UTG and MP). In cutoff with deep stacks (~100BB), Q8s is a strong opening hand: suited, good playability for straights/flushes, position advantage postflop. Standard play is to raise for value/isolation/steal blinds. Raising to 250 (2.5x BB) as I did successfully last hand maintains consistency and pot control. Folding too tight, limping poor EV.

I hold 4s 3s on the Button facing a raise to 250 from the Cutoff (Elon). While I have position and this is a suited connector, it is the lowest possible connector and prone to domination (making the second-best flush or straight). The Cutoff's range is wide, but calling invites squeezes from the blinds and leaves me with a marginal hand post-flop. With effective stacks around 100BB, the implied odds are decent, but not sufficient to justify calling with such a weak hand this early in the tournament. A fold is the standard and prudent play.

CO opens to 2.5x with BTN folding, giving us a decent price from the SB. J7s is a marginal-but-playable defend: suitedness gives postflop equity and some board coverage, and calling keeps the pot controlled while we’re out of position. I don’t love 3-betting this hand since it’s easily dominated when called and doesn’t block strong continues; if BB squeezes big we can comfortably fold.

I have A5o in BB facing a 250 raise from CO and a call from SB. Getting 4:1 pot odds (150 to win 600). A5o has good playability with ace-high potential and wheel straight draws. Easy defend from BB at this price.

We flopped a decent diamond flush draw (9 outs) but have no made hand and we’re out of position in a 3-way pot versus the preflop raiser. This board (K-T-2 with two diamonds) connects well with CO’s range, so leading risks getting raised off equity and doesn’t accomplish much multiway. Checking lets CO c-bet; then we can continue by calling reasonable sizing or occasionally check-raising as a semi-bluff depending on action and sizing.

I whiffed this K-T-2 flop completely with A5o - just ace high. Elon was the preflop aggressor and will likely c-bet. Checking is mandatory here; I have no made hand and no draw. Will fold to aggression.

Preflop, I raised Q8s from cutoff, a standard play with suited connectors for its playability and position. Flop Kc2dTd hits my diamond flush draw perfectly (9 outs, ~35% equity). Both blinds checked, showing weakness and giving me initiative in a 3-way pot of 750. As the preflop raiser, my range includes top pair (Kx, Tx), overpairs, sets, and draws. Betting semi-bluffs for fold equity, protection against their floats/completions, and value from worse draws/weak pairs. Checking risks giving free cards to overcards/straights and loses momentum. Sizing 500 (2/3 pot) balances value, protection, and pot control with deep stacks.

We have a J-high flush draw (9 outs) on Kc-Td-2d in a 3-way pot, facing a relatively small c-bet (500 into 1250). We’re getting ~3.5:1 immediate odds (need ~28.6%), and a flush draw has ~36% to improve by the river, plus some implied odds when we hit. Check-raising here is higher variance and awkward multiway (non-nut draw, can get 3-bet/jammed or run into AdXd), so the best EV line is to call and realize equity, keeping CO’s bluffs/value-bets in and preserving flexibility for turn play.

I have ace high on K-T-2 with no draws (no diamonds, no straight draw). Facing a 500 bet from Elon and a call from FiveTwo. Even with 3.5:1 pot odds, my equity is terrible here - I'd need to hit an ace which might not even be good against ranges that include AK, AT. Clear fold.

Turn 6s doesn’t improve us and doesn’t significantly change the CO’s range advantage on K-T-2 two-tone. With a non-nut J-high flush draw, our priority is realizing equity and keeping the pot manageable out of position rather than turning it into a high-variance bluff line that often runs into Kx/Tx/strong draws that continue. Checking also allows CO to keep barreling bluffs/smaller hands, giving us good implied odds when we hit diamonds, and we can respond based on sizing (call reasonable bets, fold to very large pressure when implied odds shrink).

Heads-up on turn vs SB who has checked twice (preflop call of my CO raise with Qd8s, flop call of my 2/3 pot bet into coordinated KdTd2d board hitting my diamond FD perfectly). Pot 1750, my stack deep (~94BB). Board Kc2dTd6s: FD still live (9 outs, ~20% equity), no showdown value (Qx high). SB's check-call flop range: likely weak Kx/Tx, stubborn draws (e.g., diamond draws, gutshots like J9), floats now behind. Continued aggression exploits SB's weakness, denies equity to completing draws/overcards, fold equity vs worse (pairs/draws that fold turn), builds pot for when I hit. Checking risks free river card to outs (e.g., diamond, runner-runner straights) and gives up initiative. Bet 2/3 pot (~1150, rounded to 1100 for sizing) balances semi-bluff/value from worse draws/weak pairs, maintains range advantage as preflop raiser.

We have a J-high flush draw (9 outs) with one card to come. Villain bets 1100 into 2850 (~39% pot), giving us pot odds of 1100 to win 3950 (~27.8%), which is worse than our raw river hit rate (~19.6%). However, we only need to win a bit more when we hit (roughly ~600 chips on average) to make the call profitable, and stacks are still deep enough for that to happen even though it’s a non-nut draw (some reverse implied odds vs A♦x♦/Q♦x♦).

Raising is unattractive: our draw isn’t nut, his barreling range is strong (Kx/sets/strong draws), and if he 3-bets/jams we can’t continue profitably. Best line is to call and realize equity, then reassess river (value when we make the flush; otherwise mostly check/fold vs big pressure).

River 8c is a complete brick for our Jd7d: we miss the flush and have no pair. As the out-of-position caller versus the preflop raiser/barreler, we have very little showdown value and our hand doesn’t block his Kx/Tx value region (nor does it block his diamond bluffs well enough to justify a thin lead-bluff). Leading as a bluff is also unattractive because this runout doesn’t credibly represent many strong hands for us beyond slow-played Kx/sets, and those are a small portion of our line compared to missed diamonds. Best is to check and, if he bets, respond based on sizing (mostly fold vs meaningful pressure).

River 8c gives me the pair of 8s (8s pairing board's 8c), providing decent showdown value with a Queen kicker on a K-T-8-6-2 board. No flush or straight completed that beats it obviously (possible J9 straight, but narrow; Kx/Tx beat me). FiveTwo (SB) has check-called preflop raise, flop cbet (500 into ~750 on draw-heavy diamond board), turn overbet (1100 into 1750), and now checked river—indicating capped range: weak Kx/Tx, stubborn missed draws (diamonds, guts), Ace-high floats unlikely to call triple barrels. My image as CO raiser who cbets/2nd barrels reps strong hands (KP, sets, two-pair+). Checking risks chopping or losing to bluffs, but value betting extracts from worse (pairs below 8s, draws). Bet 2/3 pot (~2600) balances value from weaker holdings/draws and thin value from stubborn Tx/6x, denies chops, builds stack in tournament. Deep effective stacks (~80BB) support polarized sizing.

River bricks and we have zero showdown value with J-high. Calling is pure torch. Bluff-raising is also poor here because Elon’s stack is short after betting (he’ll have ~3100 behind), so any raise gives him very attractive pot odds and he’s likely to station with virtually any Kx/Tx or even some underpairs; our line (check/call, check/call, check/call) is heavily weighted to missed diamonds, so we don’t represent enough strong value to generate folds. Best play is to fold and preserve our stack.

I am UTG with Jack-Trey suited. In a 6-max game, this hand is significantly below the standard opening range from early position. Playing such a weak hand out of position often leads to difficult post-flop situations, especially with aggressive players like Elon still to act. My priority at this early stage of the tournament is to maintain a solid range and avoid unnecessary risks with marginal hands.

7h4c is a complete trash hand: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with minimal post-flop potential. In middle position with 6-handed play and deep stacks, folding is mandatory to avoid speculative calls or limps that bleed chips. No position advantage or table dynamics justify entering the pot. Preserve stack for premium hands.

I am in the Cutoff with Ten-Seven offsuit (T7o). The action has folded to me. While the Cutoff is a steal position, T7o is well below the standard opening range for this position. It has poor playability post-flop and is easily dominated. There are still three players left to act (Button, Small Blind, Big Blind), making a steal attempt risky with such a weak holding. The prudent play is to fold.

Folded to me on the button with Q7o. In 6-max this is within a profitable button opening range thanks to position and immediate fold equity versus the blinds. I prefer raising over limping to pressure SB/BB and keep initiative; sizing 2.5x matches the table’s standard so far and builds a pot I can win with a single continuation bet frequently.

I have 9c5d in the small blind facing a button raise to 250. This is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability out of position. 95o is not strong enough to defend here - it's a clear fold against a button steal.

I’m in the big blind with Tc5c facing a button open to 250. I have 100 already posted so calling costs 150 (total commit 250) into a 400 pot — calling gives ~27% break-even equity (150/550). T5s has decent postflop playability (suited, straight/flush and two‑pair potential) and is worth defending vs a likely wide BTN steal. A 3‑bet to 400 is too thin here — I lack blockers/hand strength and don’t want to bloat the pot out of position early in the tournament. Plan: call to see a flop, continue with top pair or strong draws, fold to heavy aggression if I completely miss. Commit total: 250.

I completely missed this flop with Tc5c on Ah Kd Kh — no pair, no nut or flush draw (only a backdoor club possibility). The button was the preflop aggressor and his continuing range heavily contains A‑x, K‑x and strong broadway hands that dominate my holding. Out of position I don’t want to bloat the pot with air or turn my hand into a marginal bluff without blockers. Plan: check to control the pot and induce action. I will fold to any significant C‑bet and only continue if I see clear weakness (very small stab) or pick up real equity on the turn (e.g. a club).

As the preflop raiser on A-K-K rainbow, my range has a big advantage (lots of Ax, strong Kx, and overpairs) while BB has many misses and is capped somewhat by not 3-betting premium AK/KK often. This is a great spot for a small, high-frequency continuation bet to fold out hands like small pairs, suited connectors, and broadway floats that didn’t connect. With Q7o I have virtually no showdown value, so I prefer taking the cheap stab now rather than checking and conceding equity realization. If I get check-raised, I comfortably fold; if called, I’ll mostly slow down and evaluate turns (barrel selectively on good cards like Q/J/T that improve my ability to rep value).

I hold Tc5c and completely missed the Ah Kd Kh flop — no pair, only a backdoor club possibility. The BTN (preflop aggressor) has a range that is heavily weighted toward A-x and K-x which dominate me. His bet is very small (150 into a 700 pot ≈ 21% pot); calling 150 gives me ~17.6% pot‑odds (150/850), which is a cheap price to see the turn. My real equity is low (backdoor flush by river ≈ 5% plus a small chance to pair), but the bet is small enough to justify a call to try to realize equity or fold to clear aggression later. I will not raise — I have no blockers or showable equity and don't want to bloat the pot out of position. Plan: call now to see the turn (fold to a meaningful raise or large turn bet; continue only with clear improvement or a favourable action from villain). Total commit after calling: 400.

I have Tc5c on Ah Kd Kh 9d — I completely missed: no pair and only a backdoor club (≈24% to hit on the river). The button’s continuing range is heavily A‑x/K‑x and he checked the turn (showing pot‑control/weakness). Out of position with no blockers or real equity, I should not bluff or bloat the pot. Check to control the pot, realize my small equity on the river, and fold to significant aggression unless I actually improve (club or pair).

After the small flop c-bet gets called on A-K-K, BB’s range is now heavily weighted toward Ax/Kx (and some slowplays) plus a few stubborn pocket pairs. The turn 9d is not a great barreling card: it improves some of BB’s continues (9x/99) and doesn’t add credible new pressure points. With Q7o I have essentially no equity, no meaningful blockers, and no good rivers to reliably follow through after getting called again. A second barrel will mostly get called by the hands that dominate this texture (any Ace) and only folds out the weakest portion of BB’s range, making it a low-EV punt. Best is to check back, keep the pot small, and consider bluffing only on select river runouts if checked to.

I hold Tc5c and completely missed the river on Ah Kd Kh 9d 4d — no pair and my backdoor club draw missed. BTN opened pre, c‑bet a small flop and then checked the turn, which looks like pot control with Ax/Kx or a wide, often dominating range. I have essentially no showdown value and very few blockers, so my fold equity on a river bluff is low and I’d frequently get called by Ax/Kx/any pair. With a healthy stack and an 850 pot, there’s no reason to gamble a marginal bluff here. I will check to realize my equity and fold to any meaningful river bet.

River is a pure bluff-or-give-up spot with Q-high after we checked back turn. Minni’s flop call + turn check + river check range is still heavily weighted to Ax (very strong two-pair on this board), some Kx, and some hands with a diamond that now improved. The 4d completing the flush is a decent card to represent, but we have no diamond blocker, so we’re more likely to run into a diamond in their range and get snapped. Also, our turn check makes our value range thinner/less credible to the opponent, reducing fold equity. With little realistic chance to make Ax fold at a profitable frequency, the best play is to take the free showdown and conserve stack.

UTG position with 5h2d offsuit, one of the worst possible starting hands. No playability, straight potential, or suitedness. Folding is mandatory to avoid spew from early position. Preserve stack for premium spots in a tournament setting with 150 hands ahead.

I hold 9d 3h in Middle Position, which is a statistically very weak hand (offsuit, unconnected, low ranks). UTG folded. With 4 players left to act behind me, opening the pot or calling would be a negative EV play. It is early in the tournament (Hand 4), so preserving chips by folding trash hands is the correct strategy.

Folded to me in the cutoff with 8d4d. This is a dominated suited hand with poor playability versus the likely blind defending ranges (especially given Minni’s prior tendency to call). Opening it increases the chance I play a multiway pot out of position postflop or face a 3-bet with a hand that can’t continue profitably. With no dead money beyond blinds and plenty of better steal candidates, I’ll pass and preserve stack.

On the button with 67o, folded to me. This is a prime stealing spot. 67o is a reasonable hand to open from the button - connected cards with some postflop playability. Standard 2.5x raise to steal the blinds.

I’m in the small blind with pocket nines (9c9h) facing a button open to 250 (2.5bb) with ~95bb effective stacks. This is a clear 3-bet-for-value/isolation spot: Claude’s button open in 6-max is likely wide and 99 is ahead of many of his calling hands (Axs, Kx, weaker broadways) and plays well heads-up. I want to avoid playing a multiway pot out of position and to charge drawing hands, so I’m raising rather than just calling. I size to 900 total (roughly 3.5x the open) — big enough to generate folds and build a pot when I’m likely ahead, but not so large that I’m committed if I face a big 4-bet. If Claude 4-bets I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and his tendencies; if he calls I’ll proceed cautiously OOP, valuing sets/top pairs and protecting against big draws. Min-raising to 400 is too small (invites too many callers and multiway pots); calling is too passive. Raise to 900 to isolate and extract value.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Deuce offsuit. There was an open from the Button to 2.5bb and a significant 3-bet from the Small Blind to 9bb. King-Deuce is a very weak hand to defend against a 3-bet, especially when facing a sizing that indicates a strong range from Minni. I would be out of position for the rest of the hand and likely dominated by Minni's premium holdings. Folding is the only sensible play to preserve my stack.

I opened 67o from the button as a steal attempt, but Minni 3-bet to 900 from the SB. 67o is too weak to continue against a 3-bet - it doesn't have the equity to call profitably and isn't a good 4-bet bluff candidate. I'd need to call 650 into a 1250 pot with a weak offsuit hand. Easy fold.

I am in UTG position with 9d 8s (98 offsuit). This is a weak hand to open with from early position at a 6-max table. The hand lacks flush potential and, while connected, does not have enough high-card strength to justify entering the pot against 5 opponents left to act. The standard play is to fold to preserve chips for better spots.

J2 offsuit is far too weak to enter from middle position, especially as first-in. Limping creates dominated top-pair problems and poor realization multiway; raising has little fold equity vs blinds and stronger continues. Preserve stack and wait for better spots.
J4o is a weak hand even in the cutoff position. While I have position, this hand is outside standard opening ranges and not worth investing chips. Better to fold and wait for a stronger spot.

I’m on the Button with Qh7c — a marginal hand but strong in this spot because I have position and only the blinds to beat. The pot is 150 and a flat call would cost 100; instead I want to take the initiative and apply pressure to the SB/BB. I’ll open to 300 (3x BB), a standard 6-max sizing that gives good fold equity and denies the big blind correct pot odds to defend cheaply. I avoid the bare minimum raise (200) because that often invites looser calls and a higher chance of multi-way pots. Plan: if both blinds fold I win the pot; if called I’ll play in position (c-bet favorable flops, check/defend cautiously on ugly textures); if faced with a sizeable 3-bet I’ll fold Q7o — it’s not strong enough to continue out of position against heavy aggression.

I am in the Small Blind with the worst possible starting hand (3-2 offsuit). The button has raised to 300. There is no strategic justification for calling or raising with this hand, especially out of position. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Facing a button min-raise to 300 after multiple folds, with SB folding. Pot 450, calling 200 more gives excellent pot odds (~2.25:1). K5s suited has strong playability, good equity against a wide button opening range (30-50% of hands), blocks strong kings, and can flop strong draws or pairs. Defending BB wide OOP is standard, especially deep-stacked early in tournament. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is possible as a bluff but unnecessary with this hand's potential. Calling to realize equity postflop.

Flop Jh6h4s misses my Kc5c completely; king-high with negligible equity and no draws. Acting first OOP as BB defender vs button open-raiser. Deep stacks (~120bb effective). Check to pot control, induce bluffs/checks from wide button range (missed Ax, broadways, suited connectors). King blocks strong hands like KJ/KQ; can fold to most bets but consider calling small sizes for thin value/SDV. Betting air too thin OOP. Plan: check, fold to large bets, hero-call small if necessary.

I opened preflop from the button and got one caller — this flop (Jh 6h 4s) is a good spot to take the initiative. I have Q♥7♣: no pair but a backdoor heart draw and a useful Q blocker. Many of the big-blind’s flat-call hands (small pairs, broadways, some suited connectors) miss this board and will fold to pressure. Betting also denies correct odds to pure draws and preserves my positional advantage. I’ll make a standard continuation bet of ~60% pot to fold out weak hands and extract value from worse Jx/overcards that might call. If he raises I’ll fold Q7o; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate on the turn with my backdoor equity and positional control.

Facing a 400 bet into 1050 pot (~38% pot) on Jh6h4s board, which completely misses my Kc5c (king-high, no pair, no draws, wrong suit for heart flush draw). Pot odds ~3.6:1 require ~22% equity, but K5s has minimal equity vs button's wide raising range (AJ+, KQ+, suited broadways, connectors), which hits this board often (J, 6, 4 pairs, combo draws, gutshots). OOP as BB defender, check-fold is standard with air to avoid difficult postflop spots deep-stacked (~120BB). King blocker to KJ/KQo/s is minor; no reason to call/raise bluff profitably here early in tournament. Preserve stack for better spots vs aggressive button.

UTG in 6-max with 94o is too weak to open and plays poorly postflop (dominated top pairs, weak connectivity). Calling to see a flop also burns chips out of position with low realization. Best EV is to conserve stack and fold.
A8o in middle position is a playable hand. With one fold ahead of me, I should raise to 250 (2.5x BB) as a standard open. Limping would be weak and folding is too tight with an ace-high hand.

Call. 3♠4♠ is a speculative suited connector and with ~103bb stacks it's well worth seeing a flop against a standard 250 open from Claude. There are three players left to act (Button, SB, BB), so 3-betting light would either bloat the pot out of position or invite 4-bets — I prefer to realize equity cheaply. Calling 250 into a 400 pot gives good implied odds and only costs ~2.4% of my stack. Plan: evaluate the flop — proceed aggressively with strong draws or a made hand, fold to heavy committed pressure if I miss and face significant raises, and avoid bloating multiway pots out of position unnecessarily.

I am on the button with 2s 5c, which is an extremely weak starting hand. There has already been an open raise from Claude and a call from Minni. Even with positional advantage, this hand lacks any significant equity or playability in a multi-way pot. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Pocket deuces are an excellent set-mining hand with deep effective stacks (100bb+). The pot odds to call 200 into 650 are roughly 3.25:1, offering good implied odds if we hit a set (about 1 in 8.5 flops). Claude's standard 2.5bb raise from middle and Minni's flat from cutoff don't scream monsters, and there's BB dead money. Calling OOP is standard here for realization postflop; 3-betting small pairs OOP is too thin and bloats the pot unnecessarily early in the tournament. Folding is far too tight.

I am in the Big Blind with K4o facing a raise from Middle Position and two calls. The pot is 850 and I need to call 150 to see the flop, giving me excellent direct pot odds of 15%. However, K4o is a very weak hand that plays poorly in multi-way pots, especially out of position. It suffers from significant reverse implied odds (dominated King) and low playability. My equity realization will be poor against three opponents. Despite the price, the risk of losing more chips post-flop with a dominated hand outweighs the discount. Folding is the disciplined long-term play.

Preflop set-mining call with 22 OOP was standard for implied odds in a deepstacked tournament spot against a 2.5bb open and flat. However, we completely whiffed the flop: Js Ah 7h is a highly coordinated board with nut flush draws (hearts), wheel straight draws, combo draws (e.g., A9s, 89s), sets, two-pair combos, and strong Ax/overpairs in Claude's (preflop raiser) and Minni's ranges. Pocket 2s is a naked underpair with zero equity realization against their continuing ranges—no gutshots, backdoors, or blockers to key draws/strength. Multiway OOP first to act on this wet board, we have no showdown value, fold equity (no semi-bluff), or thin value. C-bet sizing would face overshrinks from aggressive opponents behind. Check-fold is mandatory to avoid spew; pots like this aren't worth punting early when ICM pressure is low and 150 hands remain.

I flopped top pair with A8o on Ah Js 7h. Elon checked to me. I should bet for value and to protect against the flush draw. With 850 in the pot, a bet of ~450-500 (roughly half pot) is appropriate to get value from worse aces and draws while not overcommitting.

Fold. I completely missed the flop with 3♠4♠ — no pair and only a runner-runner/backdoor spade possibility (needs both turn and river, ~4% to make a flush by river). Claude was the preflop raiser and now bets 450 into a 1300 pot; this is a standard c-bet that often represents Ax/Jx or other value hands. Calling 450 to chase runner-runner equity is a negative EV play and raising as a bluff out of position is too risky. I’ll preserve chips and wait for a better spot.

Facing Claude's 450 bet into 1300 (pot odds ~3.2:1 post-call) on this Js Ah 7h flop, our pocket 2s remain a naked underpair with no improvement, backdoors, or blockers to villain's strong continuing range (Ax, sets like JJ/AA/77, flush draws like KhQh+/any two hearts, combo draws like 10h8h/9h10h, overpairs). Preflop set-mining was correct for implied odds multiway deep, but we whiffed entirely—no showdown value OOP, fold equity absent without bluff blockers/semis, and thin value calling risks bloating pot vs likely overs (early tournament, ICM irrelevant). Hero call would be spew; disciplined check-fold preserves stack for better spots over 150 hands. Claude's sizing (1/3 pot) fits polarized value/protection vs draws.
Pocket Jacks is a premium hand, especially from UTG. Standard opening raise to 250 (2.5x BB) to build the pot while maintaining playability postflop. This is a straightforward value raise.

Claude opened UTG to 250 — an early-position raise representing a fairly strong range. I have 9♣6♣: a small suited connector that plays poorly out of position against an UTG opener and especially in likely multiway action behind. Calling 250 into a 400 pot requires roughly 38.5% equity to break even, which 9c6c rarely has vs an UTG range; 3-betting would be a thin bluff out of position with multiple players left to act. With deep stacks and plenty of hands ahead in the tournament, I’ll conserve chips and fold.

I am holding 4h 8s in the Cutoff position. Claude has raised from UTG to 250. This is an extremely weak hand and does not justify a call or a 3-bet against an early position open. My best course of action is to fold and wait for a better opportunity.

T6o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability, dominated equity against a typical UTG raising range from Claude. Position on button doesn't justify calling a 2.5BB open here, as post-flop we'll often be out of position or facing continued aggression. Pot odds are insufficient (250 to call into 400, ~1.6:1), and implied odds are low with this hand. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

We hold Ace-Four offsuit in the Small Blind facing a raise to 2.5bb from the Under-the-Gun (UTG) player, Claude. An UTG opening range is typically strong (containing AK, AQ, AJ, and high pairs), which dominates A4o. Additionally, we would be playing out of position for the rest of the hand. While the price to call is relatively cheap, the reverse implied odds are significant; hitting an Ace is dangerous if the opponent has a better kicker. 3-betting as a bluff is an option but risky against an UTG range. The most prudent play early in the tournament is to fold.

Claude opened UTG to 2.5bb and everyone folded, leaving us heads-up out of position versus a relatively tight opening range. T2o has very poor playability and reverse implied odds; even with a seemingly decent direct price, we’ll realize equity poorly OOP and face a lot of tough postflop spots. Best is to preserve chips and fold.

6♠4♥ is a weak, unsuited, disconnected hand with no high card and very poor post‑flop playability. I’m UTG (early) with ~100bb — this spot requires a tight open range. Limping or opening with this hand is unnecessary risk (likely out of position and vulnerable to raises), so I fold and wait for a better spot.
K3o is a very weak hand to play from middle position. It is easily dominated by better kings and has poor post-flop playability. Folding is the standard and correct play here.

Preflop in cutoff with 5c3d offsuit, a complete trash hand with no connectivity, low cards, and no suit. No steal incentive here as blinds are tight/passive so far (FiveTwo calls often but Claude in BB), and button (Pro) still to act behind but irrelevant for this spot. Deep stacks, early tournament—fold to preserve stack for premium hands. Standard discipline.

I am on the Button with T9o. While this is a marginal hand (unsuited connector), the action has folded to me. Crucially, I have folded every single hand prior to this (7 consecutive folds), establishing a very tight table image. This increases my fold equity significantly, as opponents are likely to give me credit for a stronger hand. The BB (Claude) has been aggressive, but my tight image may deter a light 3-bet. Raising gives me the chance to steal the blinds immediately or play in position post-flop with a hand that has reasonable straight potential. A standard open size of 2.5x BB is appropriate.

We’re in the small blind facing a button open, out of position with the big blind still to act. 9-4 offsuit has very poor playability, weak top-pair potential, and will realize equity badly from the SB. Calling invites the BB to come along and puts us in tough postflop spots; 3-betting is too ambitious with this specific holding. Best EV is to release and preserve stack for better defend/3-bet candidates.
72 offsuit is one of the worst hands in poker. Even at a decent price of 150 to call into a 400 pot, this hand has almost no playability or equity against a button raising range. Easy fold.

K8o is too weak a hand to open from Under the Gun (UTG) in a 6-max tournament setting. It is easily dominated by the ranges of the five players yet to act, and playing it out of position post-flop would be disadvantageous. I will fold to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

A9s is a premium suited ace hand from middle position after UTG fold. It's well above the opening range in 6-handed play (typically 22+, A2s+, K9s+, QTs+, JTs, ATo+, KJo+). Raising builds the pot with position on blinds, takes initiative, and has excellent playability postflop with nut flush potential, straight potential, and ace high. Standard size 2.5x BB (250) consistent with prior opens. Deep stacks (117bb effective) support aggressive play early in tournament to maximize stack growth. Limp/fold too passive.

I hold A3o in the Cutoff facing a raise to 2.5BB from Elon in Middle Position. Elon has been active and is the current chip leader, but an MP open still typically represents a decent range (pairs, broadways, strong aces). A3o is a weak off-suit ace that plays poorly post-flop, often dominated by better aces in the opener's range. It is not a profitable flat call. While it has some blocker properties for a 3-bet bluff, doing so against a large stack active player is high variance and unnecessary this early in the tournament (Hand 9/150). Folding is the standard and most prudent play.

7♠3♦ offsuit is far too weak to defend versus a middle-position 2.5bb open. It plays poorly postflop, is easily dominated when we make a pair, and calling invites the blinds to come along, reducing our positional edge. A 3-bet bluff is unnecessary this deep early and would burn chips versus a likely continuing range. Best decision is to preserve stack and fold.

KQo is a strong hand in the small blind facing a middle position raise. Elon has been active with raises. Rather than just calling out of position, I should 3-bet to take control and potentially win the pot preflop. A 3-bet to around 750 is standard sizing here.

I’m in the BB with A♠7♥ facing an UTG open to 250 and a SB 3‑bet to 750. Pot is 1,100 and it’s 650 to call (~37% immediate pot odds). A7o is frequently dominated by a typical SB 3‑bet range (better aces, broadways, pairs) and plays poorly multiway or out of position. A 4‑bet bluff (min to 1,250) is too thin here and I don’t want to commit chips with a marginal offsuit ace early in the tournament. Fold and preserve stack for better spots.

A9s is a strong hand facing a SB 3-bet after my MP open raise and button fold. It plays well postflop with position (Claude SB acts first), offering nut flush potential, straight draws, ace-high value, and blocker to AA/AK. Equity solid (~35-40% vs probable QQ+, AK, some bluffs/air from aggressive SB). Pot odds excellent: calling 500 into 1100 pot lays ~3.2:1 (need ~24% equity). Deep stacks (93bb eff) provide implied odds for set-mining/drawing hands, but A9s dominates worse aces. Folding too tight early in tournament (hand 9/150); 4-betting risks bloating pot OOP no, pos good but vs aggro Claude who may 5bet shove wide. Calling takes initiative back postflop, realizes equity, controls pot size. Consistent with GTO vs loose 3-bettors.

I 3-bet preflop with KQo and Elon called. The flop Th-4d-9h missed me completely, but I have two overcards and a gutshot to a straight (J). As the preflop aggressor out of position, I should c-bet at some frequency. This board is somewhat connected and could hit Elon's calling range, but a small c-bet around 1/3 pot applies pressure and maintains my aggressor image. If I get raised, I can fold.

Ad9s flops middle pair with top kicker on a T94 rainbow-ish board (two hearts). Blocks AA/AK and dominates Ax worse. Claude's SB 3-bet range (QQ+, AK, Axs, suited broadways/connectors) c-bets 1/3 pot for balance/protection, but polarized (value like TT/99/AJT, bluffs/draws). Equity ~35-45% vs range (sims back this), excellent pot odds (call 550 into 2150 for <20% needed, ~5:1). Position allows controlling pot OOP opponent, realizing equity cheaply, improving on safe turns or value betting. Raising risks overrep vs aggro Claude (history shows 3-bets, c-bets but folds to aggression); folding too nitty early (hand 9/150, deep 100bb+). Call keeps range wide, exploits implied odds if he has draws.

I hit the nut straight! K-Q-J-T-9. The Jh gives me Broadway straight but also completes a potential flush with three hearts on board. I don't have a heart, so I'm vulnerable to a made flush. However, I have the best non-flush hand. I should bet for value against sets, two pairs, and lower straights while charging flush draws. Betting around 2/3 pot is good sizing here.

Facing a large turn overbet of 1800 into a ~2600 pot on a highly coordinated board (Th 4d 9h Jh) that brings three hearts (enabling any two-heart holding to make a flush), broadway straight possibilities (QK, 8T), and two-pair combos (e.g., JT). My Ad9s gives middle pair top kicker, which was decent on flop but now has poor equity (~15-25% vs Claude's polarized SB 3bet double-barrel range: flushes like KQhh/AhXh, straights QK/AJ, sets TT/99/JJ, overpairs QQ+/AK turning into value). Pot odds ~3.4:1 require ~23% equity to call profitably, but villain's history of aggression (Hand 6 cbet success, Hand 2 multiway calls but river fold) combined with this sizing screams value; bluffs would check or smaller bet. Deep tournament (hand 9/150, 100bb+ eff stacks), preserve stack for better spots rather than realize thin equity OOP postflop—no, I have position but low SPR post-call (~3:1) commits us if he jams river. Folding exploits his potential thin value/overvalue and maintains range balance after calling flop.

K7o is a weak hand from UTG in a 6-handed game with deep stacks. Standard play is to fold marginal king hands like this early position to avoid playing out of position post-flop with a dominated hand. No reads suggest otherwise from prior hands, and preserving stack for better spots is key in a long tournament.

I hold 8s 3d in Middle Position, which is an extremely weak hand (unsuited, unconnected, low cards). With four players remaining to act behind me, opening the pot or calling is practically guaranteed to be negative EV. The standard and correct play is to fold immediately.

No raise yet, but 7♣3♠ offsuit (73o) is far below a profitable cutoff opening range. It plays very poorly postflop, is easily dominated when we make pairs, and we still have the button behind who can 3-bet plus two blinds who can defend. With no compelling exploit (e.g., extremely tight blinds), the best EV is to simply give up the blind and wait for better steal candidates.

Pocket 6s on the button with everyone folded to me. This is an easy raise spot - I have a decent hand with a pocket pair, great position, and can steal the blinds or play postflop in position if called. Standard 2.5x open.

I’m in the small blind with J♠6♠ facing a standard button open to 250. With ~99bb effective and only 200 more to call (250 total committed) the pot odds and hand playability justify defending. J6s has suited equity and a decent jack blocker, but it’s not a hand I want to 3-bet OOP and create difficult postflop spots — folding is too tight given the price. I’ll call the 200 additional to see a flop, keep the pot manageable and evaluate based on the flop texture and any action from the big blind/button. If the flop is blank or I run into heavy aggression I’ll be prepared to fold; if I hit spades, a jack, or a favorable texture I can apply pressure or extract value.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Nine offsuit (A9o). The Button (Claude) raised to 250 and the Small Blind (Minni) called. I am getting excellent pot odds (4:1) to see a flop. While A9o is strong enough to consider a squeeze (3-bet), calling is the standard play here to close the action and see a flop with a hand that performs well against wide opening and flatting ranges, especially since I will be out of position post-flop.

Flop is T♥ K♣ 2♥ and I hold J♠6♠ — no pair and no hearts. With two cards to come I cannot make a five-card spade flush (there are zero spades on the flop), and my only realistic outs are pairing a J or a 6 (or a very unlikely two-card runner to a straight). I’m out of position to the button (the preflop raiser) who’s shown aggressive value tendencies, so leading out OOP with nothing but high-card J is a losing line — it has poor fold equity and is easy to raise. I’ll check to control the pot size, planning to fold to significant aggression on the flop, call very small probe bets from passive players, and re-evaluate if checked through to the turn (looking to bluff or value-bet only if the board changes meaningfully in my favor).

I have Ace-high (As 9c) on a board of Th-Kc-2h. While I have an overcard with the Ace, this board strongly hits the pre-flop raiser's range (Claude) and potentially the Small Blind's (Minni) calling range. With Minni checking, my best play is to check as well and see how the Button reacts. Leading out into two opponents on a board that hits many of their broadway cards is unlikely to succeed, and checking allows me to potentially see a free turn or evaluate a bet from Claude.

I have pocket 6s on a Th Kc 2h board. Both opponents checked to me. My pair is below the board but I have position and the checks suggest weakness. A c-bet here makes sense for protection against heart draws and to potentially take down the pot. Betting around 1/3 pot for value/protection.

Call 300. Claude's flop bet is small (~25–30% pot) and gives good odds to see a turn for cheap. J♠6♠ has very little immediate showdown value but has backdoor spade potential and two clean pair outs (J or 6), and I don't want to fold a profitable price out of position. Raising OOP with nothing is a poor line — it creates difficult spots against both the button and the big blind. Plan: call to see the turn, fold to significant aggression if I don't improve, and if I hit a J/6 or get meaningful backdoor equity on the turn I'll reassess (possibly betting for value or turning a semi-bluff).

I am in the Big Blind holding Ace-high (As 9c) on a board of Th-Kc-2h. Following a continuation bet from the Button (Claude) and a call from the Small Blind (Minni), my hand strength is significantly diminished. In a multi-way pot, the likelihood that Ace-high is the best hand is very low, especially given that this board texture heavily favors both the pre-flop raiser's range and the Small Blind's calling range. While I am getting decent pot odds (roughly 4.5:1), I only have one overcard and no direct draws. Calling would leave me out of position for the rest of the hand with a weak holding that is difficult to play on later streets. Folding is the most prudent and disciplined play here.

Out of position on T♥ K♣ 2♥ 5♦ with J♠6♠ — no pair, no backdoor spade, only ~6 clean outs (three J’s and three 6’s) and poor realized equity. Pot is 1,350 and stacks are deep, so betting OOP with nothing is a marginal, high-variance line that’s likely to get called by Kx/Tx or raised by stronger hands. Claude’s small flop c-bet then slowing down on the turn suggests a wide/marginal range; I’d prefer pot control and to realize my equity rather than creating a big pot out of position. I’ll check the turn, fold to substantial aggression, call small-to-medium-sized bets to see the river, and re-evaluate on the river (value-bet if I pair up, consider a river bluff only with a very credible story and a favorable runout).

I have pocket 6s on Th Kc 2h 5d - an underpair to the board. Minni check-called my flop c-bet and checks again on the turn. The 5d doesn't change much. I'm likely behind if Minni has any K or T, and there's still a heart flush draw out there. Betting again risks building a pot where I'm often behind. Better to check behind for pot control and try to realize my showdown value cheaply.

Board is T♥ K♣ 2♥ 5♦ Q♥ and I hold J♠6♠ — no pair, no hearts, just J-high. Claude c-bet small on the flop then checked the turn, which is consistent with a wide/marginal range (Kx, Tx, small pairs, some missed hearts). The river Q♥ completes more heart combos and gives Claude many hands that will call a bluff (Kx, Tx, Qx, any two hearts that make a flush), and I don’t have meaningful blockers to the hands I’d want to fold out. With good pot control, deep stacks and little fold equity on this runout, the prudent line is to check and take it down at showdown if Claude also checks, or fold if he fires a sizable bet. I’ll check and preserve my stack rather than bluff into a likely calling range.

River brought Qh completing the flush draw. I have pocket 6s - an underpair to 3 overcards (K, Q, T). Minni check-called flop and has checked river. My hand has very little showdown value here and zero value to bet - I only get called by better hands (any K, Q, T, or flush). Checking back to try and win against complete air at showdown is the only sensible play.

I am in UTG position with 9c 8d (98 offsuit). This is a weak hand to open from early position due to the high likelihood of being dominated and playing out of position post-flop against 5 opponents. With a 100BB stack early in the tournament, there is no need to play marginal hands from UTG. The standard play is to fold.

8h2h is far too weak to enter from middle position. Limping invites multiway pots with a dominated suited hand and poor playability; raising would be overly loose and risks getting 3-bet or called by better ranges. Preserve stack and wait for stronger opening spots/positions.
ATo in the cutoff with two folds ahead. This is a solid opening hand in late position. I'll raise to 250 as a standard open to build the pot and potentially take down the blinds.

Cutoff opened to 2.5bb (250). I’m on the button with Q♥4♦ — a marginal, offsuit queen with very poor playability and lots of domination against a typical cutoff opening range. Calling 250 into a 400 pot requires ~38% equity to break even; Q4o rarely has that vs a standard open and will face frequent c-bets and possible 3-bets from the blinds, leaving me in tough spots postflop. 3-betting as a bluff is unattractive here — I don’t have good blockers and the hand is too weak to continue if I get reraised. Preserve my stack and fold, wait for a better spot on the button.

I am in the Small Blind with Queen-Deuce offsuit. Claude, the chip leader, has opened for 2.5x from the Cutoff. This hand is extremely weak and plays poorly out of position. Given Claude's frequent aggression and the risk of being squeezed by the Big Blind or dominated post-flop, folding is the most disciplined and profitable long-term decision.

Claude raised to 250 from cutoff after two folds, indicating a wide range including many king hands that Kc blocks. K4s is a strong suited king suited for 3-betting in BB due to fold equity on the squeeze (folds behind), good playability postflop (flush/straight potential), and protection against worse kings. Postflop OOP vs aggressive Claude (cbets frequently per history), 3betting denies equity and takes initiative. Standard sizing ~3x his raise: 750. Pot becomes 400 + 650 added = 1050; deep stacks support this. Superior EV to calling OOP or folding.

ATo facing a 3-bet from Elon in the BB. This is a marginal spot - ATo is decent but not strong enough to 4-bet for value. Folding is too tight given I have position. Calling 500 more to see a flop with position is reasonable. Pot odds are ~3:1 (calling 500 to win 1550). I'll call and play postflop in position.

Preflop 3-bet with K4s clubs as a squeeze from BB after CO open and folds behind provided fold equity and good playability. Flop 4h7dTs is decent: hits bottom pair (4s), K overcard blocker, rainbow board reduces flush draws. As preflop aggressor OOP heads-up vs Claude (aggressive history, frequent cbets), standard to cbet for thin value from Ax high, worse pairs (77/44 unlikely in call range), floats/backdoor draws, and high fold equity from missed overpairs/dominators like QQ-JJ, AKo/AQo. Board low, favors my perceived strong range (sets, two-pair, overpairs, str8 draws). Check risks free cards to OESD (56/89), gutshots (89/J9), improving overcards, or inducing unnecessary bluffs; better to charge and build pot with ~showdown value. Size 2/3 pot (1000 into 1550) balances value/protection, denies equity, deep stacks (9k eff) support. Superior to check/call or check-fold given initiative and hand strength.

I flopped top pair top kicker (tens with ace kicker) on a dry 4h-7d-Ts board. Elon 3-bet preflop and is c-betting 1000 into 1550. His range includes overpairs that beat me, but also AK/AQ that I'm ahead of. TPTK is strong enough to continue. Calling keeps his bluffs in and lets me evaluate the turn. Raising folds out worse hands and only gets action from hands that beat me.

Turn 9h is neutral-ish: pairs my 4 for bottom pair + top kicker (K blocker to broadways), no completed straights on this board (requires 5-6-8 or J-8 combos unlikely to have flat-called 3bet), but adds second heart enabling flush draws. Heads-up OOP vs Claude's flat-call range (weaker pairs like 88-JJ/55/77 sometimes, Ax high, floaty broadways, backdoor draws) after my preflop squeeze 3bet and flop cbet continuation. Hand has showdown value but vulnerable to overcards/improvers; betting thin value/protection denies equity to his missed draws (now hearts prominent), charges stubborn Ax/floats, builds pot from initiative. My range advantage as 3bettor favors continued aggression here. Size 2/3 pot (~2350) balances value (from worse made hands/floats) vs bluffs, standard post-oak sizing deepstacked (eff ~8k). Superior EV to checking OOP risking free rivers or bloated pot post-bet.

I have TPTK (tens with ace kicker) on 4h-7d-Ts-9h. Elon 3-bet pre and has now double-barreled. The 9h is relatively neutral - it completes some gutshots (J8, 86) but doesn't hit his likely 3-bet range much. His value hands include overpairs (JJ+) that beat me, but he also has bluffs and hands like AK/AQ I'm ahead of. Getting ~3.6:1 pot odds, TPTK is too strong to fold. Raising only gets called by better hands. I'll call and evaluate the river.

River 7h pairs the 7d, creating two pair combos (77, 74, 97) and trips (77), while completing the heart flush draw (4h-9h-7h). My K4c holds bottom pair top kicker (4c pairing 4h), with Kc blocker to broadways, but against Claude's check-call range on flop/turn (after facing my preflop 3bet squeeze and two continuation bets), he likely holds strong made hands like overpairs (JJ+/AA with no diamond flush blocker issues), sets (44 rare blocked, 77/Ts now trips), two pairs (T7/T9/97/74), or slowplayed straights/OESD (89 now straight). Weaker floats/Ax high would have folded to turn bet into 5k+ pot OOP. Value betting thin is -EV as BPTK loses to all his value range and gets called by flushes/straights; bluffing is poor as board heavily favors villain's range (paired low card, flush complete), low fold equity from stubborn calling station tendencies shown in history (e.g., Hand 10 check-called river). Checking preserves stack, realizes equity via showdown (beats pure bluffs/missed draws unlikely but possible in polarized range), avoids bloating pot OOP last street deep (~6k eff). Superior to betting any size given range disadvantage and board texture.

River paired the 7s and completed the heart flush. Elon checked after barreling three streets, which is suspicious. I have TPTK (tens with ace kicker) which now plays as two pair with the paired board. His check could be giving up with AK/AQ bluffs, or pot control with a medium overpair scared of the flush/paired board. Betting for thin value is risky - most worse hands fold and I only get called by 7x for trips, flushes, or overpairs that beat me. Checking back with showdown value is the safest line.

UTG 6-max with K7 offsuit is too weak and dominated to open, and limping invites isolation from aggressive players (we’ve already seen frequent opens/3-bets). With deep stacks, the reverse implied odds are high when we make a weak king. Best is to preserve stack and fold.
4s3d is a very weak offsuit hand in middle position with several players to act behind me. Easy fold.

Cutoff with Q♣4♦ — an offsuit, non‑connected, weak hand that is frequently dominated by calling or 3‑betting ranges. There are three players yet to act (button, SB Elon, BB Pro); Elon has shown a propensity to 3‑bet/attack preflop and Pro defends fairly often. Raising to steal would often put me out of position against hostile ranges, and calling 100 to see a flop is marginal because the hand plays poorly multi‑way. With ~94 BBs I can wait for a much better spot — fold and preserve my stack.

I have Ace-Nine offsuit (As 9h) on the Button. It's a strong hand in a 6-handed game, especially when folded to the button. Elon (Small Blind) and Pro (Big Blind) are the only players left in the hand. I want to raise to steal the blinds or build a pot with a superior range. A standard raise to 2.5x or 3x the big blind is appropriate here. I'll raise to 250.

3c9d is a trash hand: offsuit, unconnected, low cards with minimal potential. Facing a button raise from Flash, who has position and a wide raising range, but defending from SB OOP with this hand is -EV due to poor post-flop playability, dominated equity against likely calling ranges, and reverse implied odds. Pro still to act in BB, but no need to speculate. Standard fold to preserve stack for premium spots.

I am in the Big Blind with Q5o facing a 2.5x raise from the Button (Flash). The Small Blind has folded. The pot odds are 150 to call into a total pot of 550 (after my call), requiring approximately 27% equity. While my raw equity against a wide button range is technically sufficient (~39%), Q5o suffers significantly from poor playability and being out of position. It has reverse implied odds (weak kicker issues) and will struggle to realize its equity. Against a 2.5bb open, this hand is below the standard defending threshold (typically Q9o+ or Q4s+). Therefore, preserving chips and folding is the best long-term play.

Q9o from UTG is a marginal hand that's typically not in a standard opening range for early position. With a healthy stack of 163 BB, I should be patient and wait for better spots rather than opening weak hands from early position.

Fold. Q♠4♦ is a weak, offsuit hand with poor playability and is frequently dominated by other Qx hands. With UTG folded I still have three players behind (cutoff, button and the blinds) — opening or calling into a likely multiway pot is a marginal spot. Calling 100 into a 150 pot requires ~40% equity to break even; Q4o OOP does not meet that threshold and gives little post‑flop leverage. I conserve my 9.4k stack for better spots and avoid getting entangled out of position against aggressive raisers.

Action has folded to me in the Cutoff. A3o is a marginal hand, but in a 6-max format, it is a frequent opening candidate to exert pressure on the Button and the blinds. I will put in a standard raise to 250. This allows me to take the initiative and potentially win the blinds immediately. If I am re-raised by an aggressive player like Elon on the button, I can comfortably fold given the hand's weak playability against a 3-bet range.

Q6d suited from the button facing a CO raise to 2.5x. This is a standard defending hand due to position, suited connectivity for straight/flush potential, and deep stacks providing implied odds. Folding is too tight; 3-betting is unnecessary with this speculative hand better suited for set-mining post-flop play. Calling keeps the pot multi-way potentially with blinds behind and realizes equity effectively.

I am in the Small Blind with Tc 6s, facing a raise from the Cutoff and a call from the Button. The pot is 650 and I need to call 200, giving me roughly 23.5% pot odds. However, T6o is a very weak hand that plays poorly multi-way, especially from the worst position at the table. My equity against two opponent ranges is low, and I will likely be dominated. Folding is the standard and correct play here to preserve chips.

We’re in the big blind facing a 2.5x open from Flash and a flat from Elon, so we’ll be out of position multiway. 32o has extremely poor equity and, more importantly, terrible playability and realization in a 3-way pot. Even getting a good price (150 to win 650) doesn’t compensate for how often we’ll be dominated or forced into negative-EV postflop spots. No profitable squeeze angle with this hand either.

The flop of 5s-8h-7s is quite coordinated and generally favors the calling range of the Button (Elon), which likely contains many middling pairs and suited connectors. My A3o has very little equity on this board and no real draws. Since I am out of position against an aggressive opponent, I will check to see how he reacts. Betting here into a range that hits this board hard would likely result in me being forced to fold to a raise or a float.

Flop of 5s-8h-7s is highly coordinated with two spades and a wheel straight draw possible, plus open-enders around 6-9-10. Preflop, Q6d suited was a standard button defend with position and playability. Flash's CO raise and flop check suggests weakness or a trap, but given the board's drawiness, his checking range likely missed (no Ax, broadway often floats pre but checks here). With position, I should c-bet as a bluff/ semi-bluff to take it down, as my gutshot straight draw (to 4 for 4-5-6-7-8), backdoor flush draw, and overcard Q have decent equity and fold equity against his weak check-back range. Checking back is too passive OOP later; betting 60% pot denies equity and builds pot when ahead. Size of 400 denies draws and gets calls from worse like Jx, Tx.

The flop 5s-8h-7s is very unfavorable for my A3o, as it heavily connects with Elon's calling range from the button, which likely includes many suited connectors and middle pairs. I checked hoping to see a cheap turn, but Elon's bet of 400 into a 650 pot is substantial. Since I am out of position with only Ace-high and no significant draws on a highly coordinated board, I have very little equity. Continuing would likely lead to further losses on later streets. I will fold and preserve my stack.

UTG in a 6-max game, 94BB effective. Q♦T♦ is a playable, well-rounded hand — suited broadway with good equity and postflop playability. I want the initiative and to thin the field, so I’ll open-raise rather than limp. A standard 3x BB open (300 total) gives fold equity against the blinds, charges speculative hands, and puts me in position to C‑bet favorable flops. Plan: against a single call proceed with continuation bets on appropriate textures; vs a small 3-bet I may call and play postflop (deep stacks allow realization of equity), but I will fold to large 3-bets from tight, polarizing opponents to protect my stack.

I have K-2 offsuit in middle position facing a raise from Minni in UTG. K-2o is a very weak hand to play against an early position opener, and calling or raising would likely put me in a disadvantageous spot post-flop with poor equity and position. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Preflop, UTG raise to 300 (3x BB) from Minni, who has shown a tight-aggressive style in previous hands (e.g., 3-betting over Claude in hand 4, winning pots with aggression). Ah7h is a strong suited ace in cutoff position, with good playability, position on the raiser and blinds, and ace blocker against their AA/AK range. Folding is too tight deep-stacked (68BB); 3-betting risks isolation OOP against a strong range or multiway pots. Calling is optimal: realizes equity multiway if blinds call, controls pot size, and leverages postflop position for floats/ bluffs or value with top pair/top kicker potential. Pot odds excellent (450 pot, 300 to call).

I am on the Button with J8s facing a raise to 300 from Minni (UTG) and a call from Elon (Cutoff). While I have the advantage of position and direct pot odds are reasonable, UTG opening ranges are typically strong and often dominate a hand like J8s (e.g., AJ, KJ, QJ). Additionally, playing a suited two-gapper in a multi-way pot carries reverse implied odds risks (dominated flushes or top pair weak kicker). Without a more connected hand like JTs or T9s, the disciplined play is to fold and look for a better spot to enter the pot.

We’re in the small blind out of position versus an UTG open (3bb) and a caller, with the big blind still to act and covering everyone. 62o has very poor playability and realization multiway, and it doesn’t provide useful blockers for a profitable squeeze bluff. Calling is a long-term leak here and squeezing is too high-variance into strong ranges with a bad hand. Best is to preserve the stack and fold.

Q4o is a weak hand in the big blind facing a raise and a call. Even with pot odds of ~3.75:1, this hand plays poorly multiway and out of position. Q4 will rarely make strong hands, and when I hit a queen I'll often be outkicked. Folding is the disciplined play here.

I opened preflop and now have top pair Q-T (Q♦T♦) on a relatively dry 3♦-T♥-2♣ flop. As the preflop aggressor a continuation bet is correct: many of Elon’s calls (Ax, Kx, broadways, small pairs, suited connectors) miss this texture and fold to pressure, while I still get value from worse tens, Jx and some draws. I’ll keep the sizing modest to control the pot out of position and to preserve fold equity — a ~1/3 pot c-bet (250 into 750) extracts value and charges floats without committing too much. If Elon check‑raises big I’ll respect that line (likely sets/two‑pair) and fold or proceed very cautiously; vs a call or small raise I’ll continue to the turn with top pair and re-evaluate.

Flop is 3d Th 2c, a dry rainbow board that misses much of Minni's tight UTG raising range but hits some Tx, low pairs, or overpairs lightly. Her small 25% pot bet (250 into 1000) suggests either weak value/thin value (Tx, sets unlikely) or a probe to charge draws/continue story. With Ah7h, I have Ace-high showdown value, block strong Ax hands (AA/AK/AT), backdoor flush draw (3 hearts total), and excellent position heads-up deep-stacked (65BB effective). Folding is too tight against small bet; raising risks playing OOP against her strong range post-turn or getting called by medium strength. Calling realizes equity cheaply (~20% implied odds), controls pot, and sets up turn bluffs/floats with initiative and position if she checks. Pot odds: 1000:250 = 4.25:1, fine even for ~25% equity.

I have Q♦T♦ on 3♦ T♥ 2♣ 7♦ — top pair with the queen kicker plus a one-card diamond flush draw (I hold two diamonds and there are two on board). As the preflop aggressor who c‑bet the flop, I still have a range advantage and should continue applying pressure: a turn bet protects my hand from free rivers and extracts value from worse tens, Jx, single-pair hands and paying draws. Elon called my flop c‑bet, which is consistent with a fairly wide calling range (pairs, Tx, broadways, some suited Aces/diamonds and small pairs), so a value/protection bet is correct. I’ll size ~60% pot (≈800 into 1250) — big enough to charge draws and get value but not so large that I’m pot-committed vs a big check-raise. If Elon check‑raises large (representing sets/two‑pair) I will fold; if he calls I’ll reevaluate on the river based on the card and his action.

Turn board brings the 7d, pairing my 7h for second pair top kicker (Ah), which improves my Ace-high to a showdown hand with significant playability. Minni's UTG range remains strong (Tx, overpairs JJ+, sets), but her small flop c-bet (250 into ~1000) and now larger turn bet (800 into 1250, ~64% pot) suggests thin value with Tx (e.g., AT-AJ, KTs, suited connectors), probes, or turned draws (diamond flush draw now live with two diamonds). I block strong Ax hands like A7s-ATs. Position is excellent heads-up deep-stacked (~80BB effective). Pot odds 2050:800 = ~28% equity required; second pair has ~30-40% vs. polarized range (beats bluffs/draws, live A outs). Raising thin value risks folding out worse or stacking to better Tx/overpairs OOP for her; calling realizes equity cheaply, controls pot, keeps bluffs/draws in, and sets up river value bets if ahead or bluffs if checked to. Folding too tight early (hand 14/150) with position and improved hand.

I have Q♦T♦ on 3♦ T♥ 2♣ 7♦ T♣ — trips (three tens) with a decent queen kicker. This is a very strong hand vs most of Elon’s calling range, but it is vulnerable to full houses/quads and to single-Tx hands with A/K kickers. Elon’s line (call flop, call turn, check river) is consistent with a wide middle-strength range: missed draws, single tens, and some pocket pairs or sets that could have trapped. The river paired the ten, which makes full houses possible but still relatively limited. I want to extract value from worse tens, weaker Tx hands and medium pairs that will call a value bet, while avoiding overcommitting if Elon check‑raises big (which would usually indicate a full house). Pot = 2850; I’ll bet ~50% pot (1500) — big enough to get value from calling hands but small enough to fold to a large polarizing raise. If he flats I’m happy to go to showdown; if he check‑raises massively I’ll respect the range and fold.

River pairs the tens (Th Tc), putting trips in play for 10x hands, but my Ah7h improved to pair of 7s with Ace kicker on the turn 7d, giving me decent showdown value heads-up in position against Minni's UTG range. Her line: UTG 3x raise pre, small flop c-bet (250 into ~900) on dry 3dTh2c (suggests wide continuation or thin Tx), larger turn bet (800 into 1800ish, ~45% pot) after 7d (possible value with Tx/overpair or diamond draw), now river bet 1500 into ~2850 (~53% pot). This polarizes her somewhat: strong value (Tx like AT/KT/QT/JT for two pair/trips/overpair JJ+/sets 22/33/T10s unlikely from UTG), turned draws (diamond flush missed, 67 straight draw missed), or bluffs/value with medium strength. I block AA/AK/AT strong Ax, beat ace-high bluffs/missed draws/backdoor hearts (though I have hearts too), lose to Tx/overpairs/sets. Pot odds excellent: 2850:1500 ~2.9:1 (26% equity needed); vs her range, pair 7s/Ace has ~35-45% equity (beats bluffs/draws/A-high, live A-outs vs overpairs). Deep effective stacks (~5k), hand 14/150, position allows cheap realization. Raising thin (min 3000 risks getting called by better or shoved on); folding too tight early with showdown value and blockers. Calling optimal: realizes equity, extracts max value from worse.

I am UTG with Ace-Queen offsuit, which is a very strong hand in a 6-max format. My stack is healthy at 94.5 big blinds. I will open with a standard raise of 2.5x the big blind to build the pot and exert pressure on the remaining players, while also potentially isolating a single opponent.

94o is a trash hand preflop: offsuit, disconnected, low cards with poor playability and low equity against an UTG raising range. Flash's UTG raise to 2.5x BB indicates strength. Pot odds are 250 to win 400 (38%), but equity is insufficient (~20-25% at best vs tight range). No position or implied odds justify calling. Folding preserves my 3950 stack for better spots in a tournament setting.

I am holding 6h 2d in the Cutoff, facing a raise to 250 from the UTG player (Flash). 6-2 offsuit is one of the weakest hands in poker. Against an Under-the-Gun opening range, which is typically strong, this hand has very poor equity. Additionally, there are three players left to act behind me. There is no strategic justification for calling or raising with this holding. The only correct play is to fold immediately.

Flash opened from UTG to 2.5bb in a 6-max setup. J7o is too dominated to profitably flat here (we’ll often make second-best top pair), and it’s also a poor 3-bet bluff candidate versus an early-position range since we lack blockers and playability. With deep stacks and low urgency, the highest-EV option is to avoid marginal spots and fold.
T3o is a weak hand and I'm facing an UTG raise, which typically shows strength. Out of position with poor hand equity, this is a clear fold.

Flash opened UTG to 250 and I’m in the BB with 5♣8♣. This is a marginal but playable suited connector — not strong enough to 3‑bet out of position, but it has decent straight/flush potential and good implied odds with both stacks deep (I have ~123BB, Flash ~92BB). The pot is 400 and calling 150 more (total 250 committed) makes the pot 550, so I only need ~27% equity to justify a call. I’ll call to see a flop and then play cautiously out of position: fold to large/clean 3‑bets or heavy post‑flop pressure if I miss, and continue with reasonable equity (pairs, draws) or use positional reads to bluff later.

I have 8♣5♣ on 4♣ Q♣ 6♣ — I’ve flopped a made club flush. It’s a very strong hand heads‑up but non‑nut (any A♣/K♣/J♣/T♣/9♣ beats me). Flash was the UTG raiser preflop and checked the flop, so his range includes many hands (Qx, Ax/Kx, pocket pairs) that will pay to see another street and only a minority of his range contains higher clubs. I have position (he checked to me), so I should bet for value and protection while keeping the pot from getting uncontrollably large if I’m up against a single higher club. Betting roughly half‑pot (~300 into 550) extracts value from worse hands and charges any drawing/overcard hands. If he reacts strongly (large raise), I’ll re‑evaluate, but for now extract value with a standard half‑pot bet.

I hold Top Pair, Top Kicker with Ace-Queen on a monotone board of clubs. Minni has led out with a donk bet of 300 into a 550 pot. While the three clubs are concerning since I do not hold a club myself, my hand is significantly ahead of her range if she is betting a lower Queen, a flush draw (like the Ace or King of clubs), or a medium pair. Raising here might isolate me against better hands like a flopped flush or a set, while folding is too tight given the strength of my pair. Calling allows me to control the pot size and re-evaluate on the turn.

I currently hold 8♣5♣ and have a made club flush on 4♣ Q♣ 6♣; the 5♥ on the turn pairs my five but doesn’t hurt my flush. Flash (UTG) called my ~300 flop bet, so his calling range includes Qx, medium pocket pairs, Kx/Ax, and some clubs (including the few combos that beat me: A♣/K♣). The pot is 1,150 and there’s no bet to me on the turn, so I should extract value while charging any leftover equity. I’ll make a value bet of ~60% pot (700) to get calls from worse clubs, Qx and pocket pairs without committing the whole stack — if I face a large raise from a higher club I’ll re-evaluate on sizing and pot odds.

I currently hold top pair, top kicker (Ace-Queen), but the monotone club board is a significant concern since I do not have a club in my hand. Minni led on the flop and is now continuing her aggression on the turn. This betting pattern often represents a made flush, a set, or a strong flush draw. However, she could also be value-betting a weaker Queen or a lower pair. Given the pot odds (calling 700 to win 2550), folding is too tight here. Raising would only isolate me against hands that beat me (like a flush or a set). Calling allows me to keep the pot manageable and see what the river brings before making a final decision.

On the river I have 5♣8♣ on 4♣ Q♣ 6♣ 5♥ 8♠ — I still have a club flush (Qc-high) but it’s non‑nut (A♣/K♣ beat me). This is heads‑up versus Flash (UTG open) who’s been passive check‑calling the flop and turn, so his range contains lots of Qx, medium pairs, K/Ax and some clubs. Many of those hands will call a modest river bet; only a few combos beat me. Pot is 2550 — I want thin value and to charge worse hands without bloating the pot and inviting a big raise from the small set of hands that dominate me. I’ll make a small‑to‑medium value bet (~1/3 pot = 850). If he calls I win value from Qx/pairs/weaker clubs; if he jams/raises large I’ll put him on a better club and fold (or re‑evaluate), and if he folds I take the pot down now.

I have Top Pair, Top Kicker with Ace-Queen on a board that saw three clubs on the flop. Minni has led out on all three streets. On the river, she has bet 850 into a pot of 2550, offering me 3-to-1 pot odds (I need to be right about 20% of the time). While her triple-barrel line is often strong, the small sizing on the river could indicate a weaker Queen (like QJ or QT) or a missed draw attempting to buy the pot cheaply. Since I beat all her bluffs and her thinner value bets with weaker Queens, and the price is very favorable, I must call to see the showdown. Raising is not an option as it would only be called by flushes or sets that have me beaten.

Qs9c is a weak offsuit hand from UTG in a 6-handed game. Standard opening range from UTG excludes Q9o due to poor playability multiway, frequent domination by QTs+, KQo+, etc. With 39BB stack, better to wait for stronger hands rather than risk a wide multiway pot or tough postflop decisions. Opponents have deep stacks, increasing fold equity but position is too early for a steal with this hand. Folding preserves stack for premium spots.

I am holding King-Seven offsuit in Middle Position (MP). In a 6-max game, this hand is significantly below the standard opening range for this position, which typically starts around King-Ten offsuit or King-Nine suited. Opening with K7o risks domination by better Kings and Ace-high hands, and playing it out of position against active players like Claude on the button is EV negative. With 4 players left to act, the prudent play is to fold.

We’re in the cutoff with 85o, no raise in front (pot is just blinds). This hand has poor high-card value, weak playability when called (often dominated), and doesn’t realize equity well multiway. With a strong-stacked, capable button behind and active blinds, an open or limp is likely to get defended/isolated and force tough postflop spots. Best EV is to pass and wait for a better steal/iso candidate.

On the button with 84 suited clubs. Three players already folded, so it's folded to me. This is a prime stealing spot - I have position against just the blinds. 84s has decent playability post-flop if called (can make flushes/straights), and I can often take down the pot with a c-bet. Standard button steal raise to 250.

I’m in the small blind with A♣4♠ facing Claude’s button open to 250. This is an offsuit ace with a weak kicker and I’ll be out of position post‑flop — likely dominated by Claude’s opening range. Calling 200 into a 400 pot (~33% pot odds) leaves me with difficult OOP postflop decisions; a 3‑bet would be a light, speculative bluff that I don’t want to risk here against a competent opener and a live big blind to act. Preserve chips for a better spot — fold.

I am in the Big Blind with Ace-Ten offsuit. Claude, the button, has raised to 250. Claude has been playing very aggressively throughout the tournament, opening many pots. Ace-Ten is significantly ahead of a typical button opening range, especially from an active player like Claude. While I could flat call to see a flop, 3-betting here allows me to take the initiative, potentially win the pot immediately, and charge him for playing a wide range in position. I will raise to 750, which is a standard 3-bet size (3x his open).

Flash 3-bet to 750 from the BB against my button steal. I have 84 suited - a speculative hand that doesn't play well against a 3-bet range. Flash is short-stacked (6600), limiting implied odds. Even with position, 84s doesn't have enough equity against a typical 3-betting range, and a 4-bet bluff is risky against a stack that might just shove. Disciplined fold here.

I am in UTG position at a 6-handed table with A7s. In 6-max, A7s is typically a standard opening hand from early position, falling within the top ~15-20% of hands. While there is a risk of being 3-bet by the aggressive players (like Claude or Flash) or shoved on by the short stack (Elon), the hand has solid playability post-flop due to nut flush potential and Ace-blocker value. A standard open size of 2.5bb (250 chips) fits the table dynamics established in previous hands.

UTG open to 2.5bb from Pro represents a reasonably strong range. Th4c offsuit has poor playability, is frequently dominated when we make a pair, and doesn’t realize equity well multiway with position still to act behind (CO/BTN and blinds). No good squeeze/bluff candidates either given hand quality. Best is to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

K3s in the cutoff facing a UTG raise. K3 suited is a marginal hand - too weak to 3-bet for value against UTG's typically strong range, and calling risks being dominated by better kings. With position but facing early position aggression, folding is the disciplined play.

Pro opened UTG to 250. I have 9♣5♥ on the button — a weak, easily dominated offsuit hand vs an early-position raiser. With the SB and BB still to act this will often become multiway, which worsens my postflop equity and makes calling 250 a marginal play. A 3-bet bluff here is too risky against Pro’s EP range and would bloat the pot I’m unlikely to win. I’ll preserve my healthy stack and fold.

I am in the Small Blind with 4h 5c, which is a very weak hand. Pro raised from UTG, which typically indicates a strong range. Calling from the SB out of position with such a weak hand is a losing play in the long run, and there is still the Big Blind (Elon) to act behind me. I will fold to preserve my stack.

Preflop in BB facing an UTG raise to 250 from Pro (9550 stack). My holding 8h6d is offsuit, poorly connected, with low card strength and poor postflop playability. UTG range is strong (TT+, AQ+ typically in 6-handed). Calling 150 into 400 pot offers ~3.7:1 odds, but equity vs range is ~30-35%, not compensating for reverse implied odds and domination risks. No squeeze incentive as blinds folded and stacks are deep. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots.

6-handed UTG with AKo is a clear value open. Limping invites multiway action and reduces our ability to realize equity; raising builds the pot when we’re likely ahead of calling ranges and gives us initiative versus the blinds and late positions. Use the table’s standard sizing to keep our range consistent and deny cheap realizations.
95 offsuit is a very weak hand, especially facing an UTG raise. No reason to call here - easy fold.

UTG (FiveTwo) opened to 250 from early position. My hand 7♣5♥ is a weak, offsuit, unconnected holding that is frequently dominated by an UTG opening range and plays poorly multiway. Calling 250 into a 400 pot would put me in difficult post‑flop spots with little equity or fold‑equity; a 3‑bet bluff here is thin without blockers and against an early‑position raiser. With a healthy 14,500 stack I’ll preserve chips and wait for a better, more actionable spot.
