Calling invites multiway trouble and raising into several deep stacks increases the chance we get 3-bet or called and have to navigate out of position with a dominated hand. Best EV is to preserve stack and wait for better spots.
K5o is a weak offsuit hand in middle position. With 4 players still to act behind me, this hand doesn't have enough equity to profitably open-raise. Easy fold.

Cutoff with A♥8♥ is a good opening hand — suited ace with a strong blocker and good post‑flop playability. There’s no raise yet (pot 150, to call 100), so I want to take the initiative and steal the blinds/isolate the button. I’ll raise to 3×BB (300) — large enough to gain fold equity but small relative to my 7,150 stack. Plan: c‑bet good flops (ace/heart/draws), play for position; fold to large 3‑bets that shrink my implied odds.

I am on the Button with 4s 2d, which is a very weak hand. Minni has raised from the Cutoff to 300. There is no justification for calling or raising with such a low-equity hand, especially against an open from a late position player. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

43o is one of the worst possible hands: low, offsuit, disconnected, no speculative value. Facing a Cutoff raise from Minni (6850 stack, ~68bb), who has been aggressive historically but whose range includes strong hands. I'm in SB, out of position postflop, pot 450 to call 250 (effective ~half pot odds but poor implied odds with this hand). Equity crushed vs typical CO open range. BB still to act but unlikely to induce fold. Folding preserves my 80bb stack for better spots. No history this hand justifies a defend.

I am in the Big Blind with 2c 8d, facing a raise to 300 from Minni in the Cutoff. The pot odds are decent (calling 200 to win a total pot of 650, roughly 31%), but 82o is one of the worst starting hands in Hold'em. It has poor playability and equity, even against a wide late-position opening range. Playing this hand out of position is expected to be negative EV. Therefore, the correct decision is to fold.
AQo is a strong hand in UTG position. With 150BB effective, I should open raise to around 2.5x. Standard opening raise here.

Claude opened UTG to 250 (pot 400); calling costs 250. I hold T5o — a weak, poorly connected offsuit hand that is heavily dominated by a typical UTG opening range. Calling 250 into a 400 pot requires ~38.5% equity to break even heads-up (250 / (400+250)), which T5o does not have vs an UTG range. I'm out of position with deeper stacks and aggressive players behind who can 3-bet or isolate; there's no good reason to gamble with this holding. Preserve chips for better spots and fold.

I am holding pocket Kings, the second-best starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Claude has opened from UTG to 250. I need to 3-bet here for value and to protect my hand. A 3-bet also helps isolate the original raiser and prevents the players behind me (Button and Blinds) from seeing a cheap flop with speculative hands. Given the stacks, a raise to 800 is a standard and effective sizing.

Preflop on the button with Qc8d offsuit, facing an UTG open from Claude (tight-aggressive player based on history) to 250 and a 3-bet from Flash in the cutoff to 800. Pot is 1200, calling 800 into it gives poor implied odds with a marginal hand lacking playability, strong blockers, or domination potential against likely premium ranges (AA-JJ, AK, AQ+ from both). 4-betting is too thin without a polarized range here, and my stack is deep but not warranting a speculative call given multiway potential with blinds behind. Standard fold to preserve stack for better spots in this tournament phase.

I am in the Small Blind with 74o, facing an opening raise from UTG and a 3-bet from the Cutoff. This is a very weak hand in a high-pressure situation. I have no reason to get involved. The pot odds are poor and my hand has very low equity against the ranges of an UTG opener and a CO 3-better. Folding is the only viable option.

We’re facing an UTG open plus a cutoff 3-bet, and we’re out of position with Q8o on a 26bb stack. Calling 700 creates a bloated pot with poor playability and very little postflop maneuvering room, and Claude can still come along/4-bet, making it even worse. A shove would need a lot of fold equity versus two ranges that are typically strong here, and Q8o performs badly when called. Preserve the stack and wait for a better spot.

AQo is too strong to fold against a 3-bet. Flash has been active. With deep stacks (147bb effective), calling is better than 4-betting since AQo can be dominated by AK and we'd hate to face a 5-bet. Calling 550 into a pot of 1750 gives us good odds to see a flop and play our hand in position.

Missed the flop completely with AQo on Jd3c4d. I have two overcards but no pair or draw. I'm OOP against the preflop 3-bettor. Standard play is to check and let Flash act. I can reevaluate if they c-bet - my overcards have some equity but leading into the aggressor here doesn't make sense.

I have pocket Kings on a Jack-high board. This is a very strong position. Claude checked after calling my pre-flop 3-bet, which usually indicates a marginal hand like a Jack or a pocket pair, or potentially a draw. I will bet about half the pot to get value from hands like AJ, KJ, or lower pocket pairs, while also charging any diamond flush draws.

Missed the flop completely with AQo on Jd3c4d. Flash 3-bet preflop and is now c-betting 900 into 1750. I'm OOP with just two overcards - no pair, no draw beyond backdoor possibilities. The board texture favors the 3-bettor's range (JJ, QQ, KK, AA, AJ, KJ, QJ all connect). Floating OOP with overcards is generally -EV, and I'd need about 34% equity to call profitably. With just ~13% to improve on the turn and being OOP against an aggressive 3-bettor, disciplined fold is correct here.

UTG with K♠T♣ is marginal but playable in a 6-max game. I want initiative and to avoid limps, so I’ll open-raise to 300 (3x BB). That sizing is large enough to discourage multiway callers and isolate the short SB while keeping plenty of post‑flop maneuverability with my ~73BB stack. Plan: c-bet favorable flops (K/T/x or dry boards) and fold to heavy 3-bet pressure from deep stacks (KTo is often dominated); call or jam decisions vs a SB shove will be made based on pot odds and shove size.

I am holding Qc 6s in middle position. Minni has raised from UTG to 300. Q6o is a very weak hand that is significantly behind an UTG opening range. Even though I have a substantial chip lead, there is no strategic reason to call or raise with such a poor hand against early position strength. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

J9s is a strong suited connector with good playability, straight and flush potential. In cutoff position facing an UTG raise from Minni to 300 into a 450 pot, the odds are favorable (calling 300 to win 750, ~2.5:1 direct + implied odds from deep stacks). Multiway pot possible with effective stacks around 70BB+, and my position allows controlling postflop. Raising risks isolating with a marginal hand against UTG range; folding is too tight late. Call to realize equity.

I hold 9s 5d on the Button. Facing a raise to 300 from Minni (UTG) and a call from Elon (Cutoff), I have a very weak offsuit hand. The UTG opening range is typically strong, and with a caller in between, entering the pot with 9-5 offsuit is significantly negative EV. My stack is healthy at 7800 (78bb), so there is no desperation to play junk hands. Folding is the only standard play here.

We’re in the small blind with 5♠2♦, facing an UTG open to 300 and a cutoff flat, with the big blind still behind. This hand has very poor playability multiway and we’ll be out of position for the rest of the hand. Calling 250 more is a low-EV spot (dominated/rarely makes strong enough hands), and a squeeze bluff would need to be large (≈1100–1400) which risks a big portion of our ~25bb stack versus strong UTG ranges and a caller, plus BB can wake up. Best decision is to preserve chips and wait for better spots.

I have Qs8s in the BB facing a 300 raise from Minni and a call from Elon. I need 200 to call into a pot of 750, giving me 3.75:1 odds. Q8 suited has decent playability - flush potential, can make straights, and can hit top pair. With these pot odds closing the action, this is a clear call.

I flopped middle pair (8s) with a queen kicker on a very dry 8c2s3h board. This is a decent spot for me - the board texture doesn't connect well with typical raising ranges. I'll check to see what Minni and Elon do. If they check through, I can potentially value bet later streets. If Minni c-bets, I can evaluate whether to call or raise based on sizing.

Flop is 8♣ 2♠ 3♥ — a very dry, low, uncoordinated board. I opened UTG with K♠T♣ so I still have the best single overcard and a backdoor club. Against Elon and Claude’s wide calling ranges, a continuation bet will frequently take this pot down or get value from worse one-pair/overcard hands. Pot is 950; I’ll make a small-to-medium c-bet (~1/3 pot = 300) to apply pressure while keeping the pot manageable with deep stacks. Plan: fold to a large (polarizing) raise, call small or single calls and then proceed cautiously on the turn — only barrel if I improve or the turn meaningfully helps my range.

J9s misses the 8c-2s-3h flop completely: no pair, no flush draw (only backdoor spade), no straight draw (no gutshot or open-ender). Pure air with overcards. Facing UTG Minni's standard c-bet sizing (1/3 pot) on a dry, low, uncoordinated board that favors their raising range (pairs 66+, Ax suited/broadway). Claude's check suggests weakness from BB limp range. Direct pot odds ~4:1 are decent but equity realization low (~20-30% vs Minni's range), poor reverse implied odds postflop if called. Raising as a bluff risks multiway action or isolation OOP; check-fold optimal to preserve stack for better spots. Deep effective stacks (70+BB), fold equity better saved. Consistent with GTO: speculative call preflop becomes fold to c-bet on miss.

I have middle pair (8s) with queen kicker on a very dry 8c2s3h board. Minni's small c-bet of 300 into 900 could be either a value bet with an overpair or a standard c-bet with overcards. Getting 4.2:1 pot odds (300 to win 1250), this is an easy call with middle pair. The board is so dry that my hand is often good, and I can re-evaluate on the turn. Raising would turn my hand into a bluff against Minni's value range.

The Qh turn is perfect for me - I now have top two pair (queens and eights). This is a very strong hand on this dry board. I should lead out for value since Minni might check back weaker holdings if I check. Betting around half pot (700-800) looks like I'm trying to represent the Q or take a stab, which could induce calls from overpairs or one-pair hands. If Minni has an overpair like JJ/TT/99, they'll likely call. If they have AA/KK, they might raise which I can then re-evaluate.

I opened UTG and c-bet the very dry flop; Claude called. On the turn (Q♥) he leads for 750 into a 1550 pot (would be 2300 if I call) so I must call 750 for ~32.6% equity. I have no pair — only 6 clean outs (3 Kings + 3 Tens) to improve on the river (~13% chance), well below the required equity. Claude’s line (call flop → bet turn) looks like a value range (Qx or small pair) rather than a frequent bluff, and raising as a bluff here is thin vs his deep stack. With 67bb effective this is a marginal bluff‑catcher that doesn’t get the odds — fold and wait for a better spot.

I am dealt Ace-Jack offsuit (As Jc) in the Under The Gun (UTG) position. In a 6-max game, AJo is a solid opening hand even from the earliest position. My stack size is currently the largest at the table, allowing me to play aggressively. I will put in a standard open raise of 2.5x the big blind (250 chips) to take control of the pot and narrow the field.

Preflop, facing an UTG raise to 250 from Flash, who has a deep stack of 19,850 and plays aggressively at times but UTG opens indicate a strong range (likely premium hands, big pairs, big aces, etc.). My hand is 8s5h, a complete trash hand with no high card value, poor connectivity, and offsuit. In middle position with 5 players behind, including the button (FiveTwo short at 2550), SB (Claude deep), BB (Minni), and CO (Pro), calling would be spewy with dominated equity and reverse implied odds. Raising is suicidal without any bluff equity here. Folding preserves my 7725 stack for better spots in a long tournament (51/150 hands). Standard fold.

I hold Qc9c in the Cutoff facing a 2.5x raise from the UTG player (Flash), who is the chip leader. While Q9s is a playable hand and I have position, calling an UTG open leaves me vulnerable to domination (by AQ, KQ, A9s, etc.) and reverse implied odds. Additionally, there are players yet to act, including a short stack on the button who might squeeze. Given the stack depth (78BB) and the strength of an UTG range, the disciplined play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

Flash is opening UTG to 2.5bb and we’re on the Button with 4♠2♦, which is far below any defend/3-bet threshold versus an early-position range. With ~25bb effective, there’s no profitable bluff-shove or 3-bet line here; we’d be dominated, have terrible playability, and don’t realize equity well even in position. Preserve stack for better spots.

T2o is a very weak hand. Flash raised from UTG which typically indicates strength. I'm out of position for the entire hand and would need to call 200 into a 400 pot. Even with decent pot odds, T2o plays terribly out of position against an UTG range. Easy fold.

Facing a UTG open to 250 (Flash) and everyone folded to me in the big blind with 7♣2♠. This is the single worst hand in hold'em — almost no equity versus an early-position opening range and terrible postflop playability. Pot is 400 and it costs 150 to continue (total commit would be 250), but with 66 BB effective there’s no reason to spew chips calling out of position with this hand. 3-bet bluffing here is unnecessary against an UTG opener who likely has a strong range. I’ll fold and wait for a better spot to pick up chips.

UTG position in 6-handed play with K9d suited, a strong playable hand in my opening range. Standard open-raise sizing of 2.5x the big blind (250) to build the pot and take initiative. My stack is deep at ~77BB, allowing for post-flop flexibility. History shows consistent 250 opens working well. No action yet, pot 150 with blinds posted. Folding too tight, limping unnecessary in modern play.

I am holding Pocket Deuces in Middle Position facing a raise from the Under-the-Gun player (Elon). UTG opening ranges are typically strong (big pairs, strong broadways). While my stack of 78BB allows for set-mining mathematically (implied odds), the position is poor. I have four players left to act behind me, increasing the risk of a squeeze (3-bet) or a multi-way pot where 22 is difficult to play post-flop without hitting a set. Additionally, the Cutoff is short-stacked and might shove. Pocket Deuces are generally a fold in MP vs UTG in 6-max GTO strategies. The disciplined play is to fold and wait for a better spot.

UTG opened to 2.5bb and we’re in the cutoff with J6o at ~25.5bb effective. Versus a likely stronger UTG range, J6o performs poorly (dominated by better jacks, weak playability), and calling invites tough multiway spots with the button and blinds behind. A 3-bet bluff is also unattractive at this stack depth because UTG can continue aggressively (including jams) and we don’t have blockers or equity to justify it. Best EV is to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

T9o on the button facing a UTG raise of 250. This is a marginal hand but playable in position. I have good implied odds with straight potential (straights with 8-7, J-8, Q-J). My position advantage post-flop makes calling reasonable here. Stack is healthy at 150bb. Calling to see a flop in position.

Elon opened UTG to 250 and the button (Claude) has already called, so this will be a multiway pot. I have K♠5♥ in the small blind — a weak, easily dominated king with a poor kicker and little post‑flop playability out of position. Calling 200 more (250 total) would put me OOP against at least one caller and against UTG’s fairly strong opening range; a 3‑bet bluff here is too speculative and likely to get me into a bloated pot. Pot odds (~3.3:1) don’t overcome the equity disadvantage. I’ll fold and preserve my stack for a better spot.

I am in the Big Blind holding 4c 2c. Elon (UTG) has raised to 250 and Claude (Button) has called. The current pot is 650, and it costs me only 150 more to call. This gives me pot odds of approximately 4.3 to 1. While 42s is a very weak hand and I will be out of position for the rest of the hand, the price to see a flop is extremely favorable. I am the chip leader and can afford the small investment to potentially flop a monster or a strong draw in a multi-way pot.

I have middle pair (fours) on a very dangerous monotone board (3s 4s 8s). I do not hold a spade, meaning any spade in my opponents' hands has significant equity against me, and any existing flush completely crushes me. Since I am out of position against the pre-flop aggressor (Elon) and another caller (Claude), the most prudent play is to check and see how they react to the flop. Betting would likely only be called by better hands or draws with high equity.

As the preflop raiser from UTG with a strong opening range, I have significant fold equity here on a draw-heavy flop (3s4s8s, flush draw and straight draws possible). My K9dd missed completely but has king-high showdown value and backdoor diamond draw. Flash checked in BB (weakness), Claude called wide from button. Pot is 800, betting 50% pot (400) charges any draws (spades, 5x, 65s, etc.), protects against turn bricks/improvements, and takes advantage of initiative. Multiway pots check down often in history, but c-bet reps overpairs/sets. If called, re-evaluate turn with position disadvantage but stack depth (75BB). Goal: build stack by stealing dead money.

Monotone spade board (3s 4s 8s) and I have T9 with no spades. I have zero flush equity and only a gutshot straight draw (need a 7). Even if I hit my straight, any spade holder beats me. With no pair, no flush draw, and minimal equity (~8% for the gutshot), this is an easy fold. The 400 call into 1200 pot requires ~25% equity which I don't have.

I have middle pair (fours) on a monotone board (all spades), but I do not hold a spade myself. This makes my hand extremely vulnerable to any spade in my opponent's hand. Elon, who raised from UTG, is betting into two players on this board. Even if he doesn't have a flush yet, any single spade (like the Ace or King of spades) has significant equity against me. Additionally, I am out of position for the rest of the hand. While the pot odds are 4:1, the likelihood of my hand being best or improving to the best hand without a spade is low. It is safer to fold and wait for a better spot.

I am in UTG position with 8d 4s, which is an extremely weak hand (unsuited, unconnected, low cards). Opening from UTG requires a strong range due to the positional disadvantage and the number of players left to act. This hand falls well below the bottom of any reasonable opening range. Therefore, the correct and automatic play is to fold.

6-handed with no ante and UTG folded, Q9s is a standard open from middle position: it has decent high-card value, good playability, and can win the blinds often. Limping invites multiway pots versus stronger ranges, and folding is too tight given our position. With ~25.5BB, a slightly larger than min-raise size pressures the blinds while keeping our SPR workable if called and still allows us to fold comfortably versus strong 3-bets from the stacks behind.

3c7c is a weak suited hand. Facing a raise from FiveTwo with players still to act behind me (including Flash with a big stack in the blinds who could squeeze), this is not a profitable spot to call. Fold and wait for a better opportunity.

I have K♦3♠ on the button — a marginal hand with a very weak kicker. FiveTwo opened to 250 from middle position; I don't want to 3-bet with K3o (dominated too often) and folding is too tight given my position and healthy stack. Calling 250 (~3.8% of my 6,550 stack) lets me see a flop in position cheaply. Postflop plan: continue when I connect (top pair with caution due to kicker), otherwise fold to significant aggression and use position to realize equity or take the pot on later streets.

I am in the Small Blind with Ace-Eight offsuit. FiveTwo (Middle Position) raised to 250, and Minni (Button) called. I have a massive chip lead with nearly 20,000 chips. The cost to call is 200 more (to match the 250 total) into a current pot of 650, giving me 4.25:1 immediate pot odds. While A8o is a marginal hand to play out of position, the price is too attractive to fold. I will call to see a flop, but I must remain cautious as FiveTwo is a short stack (2,300 total) and could easily commit the rest of his chips on many board textures.

Preflop in BB with 4c6c, suited connectors. Facing a raise from FiveTwo (loose-aggressive MP raiser, short stack 2300) and calls from Minni (BTN, calls wide) and Flash (SB, deep and calls frequently). Pot 850, calling 150 more for total 250 committed (already posted 100 BB), offering excellent pot odds of ~15% needed equity, which 46s exceeds multiway vs typical calling ranges. Postflop implied odds strong if I hit straights, two-pair, flush draws (suited). Multiway pot favors speculative hands. History shows FiveTwo raises wide, often gets called/shows medium strength; no reason to fold good price. Cannot raise profitably with this hand. Will see flop cheap and play position (act 2nd postflop). Goal: maximize stack long-term by realizing equity in favorable spots.

I am first to act on a flop that heavily connects with broadway ranges. With only Ace-high and no significant draw on a King-Queen high board, I have very little equity against three opponents. Betting would be a low-frequency play here given I'm out of position against the pre-flop raiser and two other players. I will check and likely fold if there is significant betting action behind me.

Flop Ks Qh 2h misses my 46c completely—no pair, no straight draw (gutshot unlikely), no flush draw (board hearts heavy, my suit clubs dry). Multiway pot (FiveTwo aggressive raiser/short, Minni/Flash loose callers) with KQ high board favors value hands and draws. Bluffing here thin; low fold equity multiway, position weak (act early postflop). Check to pot control, realize equity if missed by opponents or induce bluffs later. Preflop defense was correct for implied odds; now play passively with air to minimize losses long-term.

Multiway (4 players) on K♠ Q♥ 2♥, our Q♦9♦ is a medium-strength made hand (second pair, weak kicker) with some backdoor diamond equity but no immediate draw. In a 4-way pot, a c-bet gets called by many better hands (most Kx, strong Qx) and by heart draws, while folding out a lot of the air we already beat—so betting mainly builds a pot where we’re often behind and risks a check-raise from the blinds. With a low SPR (~2), pot-control and realizing showdown value is higher EV: we take the free card, keep weaker Qx/air in, and can respond more accurately on turns (improve on 9/diamond, re-evaluate versus pressure on hearts/aces/jacks).

I flopped top pair (K♦) on K♠ Q♥ 2♥ in a multiway pot. My kicker is very weak and there are two hearts on board, so there are both Kx combos that dominate my kicker and heart draws that I want to charge/protect against. In position and facing checks from the preflop raiser, a modest continuation bet is the right play: it extracts value from Qx/smaller pairs and charges draws while keeping the pot controllable. I’ll bet ~1/3 pot to achieve that and re-evaluate on the turn — if I face a large raise I’ll assume I’m likely behind and fold.

On this board of King-Queen-Deuce, my Ace-high has very little equity. Minni is betting into three opponents on a texture that heavily favors a calling range from the button or the pre-flop raiser's range. I have no draws and no pair. Even though I have a massive chip lead, there is no reason to put more chips into a pot where I am likely far behind and have very few ways to improve. I will fold.

Flop completely missed my 4c6c: no pair, no straight draw (KQ2 board leaves no realistic gutshot or open-ender), no flush draw (clubs off-suit to board hearts). Minni's 350 bet from BTN after three checks into ~1000 pot is polarizing: value with top pair+, sets, or combo draws (hearts, Q2, K2 unlikely but possible); bluffs possible but fold equity low facing FiveTwo behind. Pot odds ~4:1 (350 into 1350) require ~20% equity, but hand has <5% vs Minni's range. Calling commits to tough turn OOP multiway if FiveTwo continues. Raising a pure bluff too thin multiway with short-stack FiveTwo who may shove. Previous check pot-controlled effectively; now fold to realize 0 equity hand and preserve deep stack (8025) for premium spots in tournament. Long-term EV: avoid bleeding chips in unfavorable postflop dynamics vs aggressive field.

Minni’s 350 into 1350 is a small stab after we checked in a multiway pot. Now that the blinds folded it’s heads-up, and we’re getting a very good price (need ~21% equity). With Q♦9♦ on K♠ Q♥ 2♥ we have second pair with showdown value; Minni can be betting a wide range here (heart draws, some Tx/Jx floats, weaker Qx, and occasional Kx/strong Qx for value). Raising mostly gets action from better (Kx, AQ/KQ/QJ) and forces worse hands/bluffs to fold, and with our short stack we’d be committing ourselves in a spot where our hand doesn’t want to play for stacks. Calling keeps bluffs in, realizes our equity, and lets us make a clearer decision on the turn based on runout and sizing.

We’re out of position with a medium-strength one-pair hand (Q9) in a pot where Minni’s flop stab can still be very wide (heart draws, some Kx for value, Qx, and occasional floats). With the pot already 1700 and our stack only 1950, leading turns tends to (1) get called/shoved on mostly by better hands and (2) fold out a lot of the bluffs/worse Qx we want to keep in. Checking keeps the pot manageable, induces bluffs, and lets us make a cleaner river decision based on her sizing and the runout.

I have top pair (K♦) but a very weak kicker (3♠). After I c-bet 350 on the flop FiveTwo called; we’re now heads-up on a K♠ Q♥ 2♥ 5♦ board with two hearts out there, so there are still heart-based river scares (someone with two hearts can make a flush on the river). The turn didn’t improve my kicker and didn’t change the range much, but I want to charge Qx and heart combos and deny a free river card. I’ll bet about half the pot to extract value and keep the pot manageable against a short-stacked opener — bet 850 chips. I’ll re-evaluate if he shows significant aggression on the river (and fold to a shove unless I have a specific read he’s shoving light).

Minni’s flop stab was very small in a 4-way pot, which can still be a wide range (many heart draws, some Kx/Qx for value, and occasional air). The turn 5♦ is mostly a blank for the board texture, and her 1/2-pot 850 barrel applies pressure, but we’re getting strong immediate odds: 850 to win 3400 (~25%). With Q♦9♦ we still have clear showdown value versus missed hearts/air and some worse Qx, plus a few clean improving outs (9 for two pair; running into Q is less useful vs better Qx but still helps vs Kx). Raising/shoving doesn’t make sense with this stack depth because we mostly get called by better (Kx, strong Qx, sets) while folding out the bluffs/draws we want to keep in. Best play is to call and re-evaluate river based on runout (especially hearts/overcards) and sizing.

River A♠ is a bad card for our Q9 (we drop to 3rd pair) but it’s an excellent card for Minni to bluff on after betting flop/turn—she can credibly represent Ax (and some strong Kx) and continue applying pressure. With our stack only 1100 into a 3400 pot, leading doesn’t make sense: a shove gets called mostly by better hands and folds out the missed heart draws/air we want to keep in, while a small blocker bet just invites a jam and puts us in a worse spot. Checking maximizes the chance she checks back missed draws, and if she does bet we’ll be getting a very good price to look her up.

I have top pair (K♦) with a very weak 3 kicker on Ks Qh 2h 5d As. FiveTwo called my flop and turn bets and now checked the river — that line is consistent with either a marginal made hand (5x, a small pair), a Kx with a better kicker, or an Ace that now beats me. The Ace on the river is a bad card for my hand: a river bet will mostly fold out the very weakest hands while getting called by the hands that actually beat me (Ax, Kx with better kickers), so value-betting is thin/negative EV. I don't want to build the pot with a vulnerable top pair — checking to showdown avoids risking more chips and is the prudent line here.

We’re UTG 6-handed with ~11BB (1100) and J6o, a hand that is frequently dominated (by AJ/KJ/QJ/JT, etc.) and plays very poorly postflop, especially out of position. With this stack depth the profitable UTG strategy is mostly raise/fold or shove with a tighter range; limping burns EV and invites isolation from big stacks behind. Best decision is to preserve chips for a stronger shove/open spot.

Pocket 2s in middle position. Small pairs play well for set mining with deep stacks. I have a healthy stack at 148bb. Standard open to 2.5x to build a pot while keeping the investment small. If I get 3-bet, I can fold. If I see a flop, looking to hit a set which has roughly 12% chance.

A9s in the cutoff vs a standard MP open is a 3-bet candidate: it has a suited Ace (good postflop playability and blockers to AK/AQ) and will fare well heads-up. I want to take the initiative, apply pressure to the original raiser and charge the button/blinds for speculative calls. With ~85bb effective I can comfortably play postflop if called. I’ll 3-bet to a sizing (~900) big enough to discourage loose callers/squeezes but keep postflop playability. Plan: if I get isolated we play heads-up with initiative and c-bet favourable textures; if faced with a large 4-bet I will fold to preserve my stack; if faced with a small 4-bet I’ll re-evaluate based on opponent and sizing.

I am holding AsJs on the button, which is a premium suited ace. Claude opened from middle position, and Minni 3-bet from the cutoff. Minni has been active and aggressive in previous hands, suggesting her 3-betting range might be wider than just premium pairs. While 4-betting is an option, calling allows me to use my position to play a post-flop game with a hand that has great playability. Folding is too conservative given the strength of my hand and my position. I will call to see a flop, keeping the pot manageable and retaining the ability to outplay my opponent on later streets.

Preflop in SB with 4h9c, a complete trash hand with no playability, low cards, offsuit, and no straight or flush potential. Facing a raise from MP (Claude), a large 3-bet from CO (Minni), and a flat call from BTN (Flash). Pot is 2200, to call 850 OOP in a 3-bet pot. No fold equity, terrible position postflop, and opponents likely strong. Standard fold to preserve stack.

I am in the Big Blind holding Jh Tc with a stack of 7700. The action involves a Middle Position open to 250, a Cutoff 3-bet to 900, and a Button cold call of 900. I am facing a bet of 800 to call into a pot of 2200. My hand, Jack-Ten offsuit, is easily dominated by the ranges of a 3-bettor and a cold caller (e.g., AJ, KQ, KJ, AT, TT+). Furthermore, the original raiser (Claude) is still to act behind me and could 4-bet. Playing a dominated offsuit connector out of position in a multi-way 3-bet pot has significant negative implied odds. The pot odds of roughly 2.75:1 are insufficient to justify the risk. Folding is the clear correct decision.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni and a cold-call from Flash. I need to call 650 into a pot of 2200 (~30% pot odds). With pocket 2s, I'm purely set mining at ~12% to flop a set. Implied odds analysis: Minni has ~6700 behind, Flash has ~18000. If I hit my set against two players showing strength, I can likely extract significant value. Getting roughly 10:1 implied odds vs Minni's stack makes this a profitable set-mine despite being OOP.

Missed my set on Q-5-K board. Pocket 2s is essentially worthless here against two opponents who showed preflop strength (3-bet and cold-call). This board hits their likely ranges hard (AK, KQ, QQ, KK, AQ, etc.). Best play is to check and fold to any bet - no bluffing opportunity against two players on this texture.

Check. I 3-bet preflop intending to take the initiative, but both the opener and the button called so this is a multiway pot. The flop K♥ Q♠ 5♣ doesn’t hit my A9♥ — I only have Ace‑high and a very weak backdoor heart possibility. In a three‑way spot a c-bet is marginal: I’ll get called by many better hands (Kx, Qx, pocket pairs) and risk facing raises that I don’t want to fold or commit lots of chips to with no pair. Checking controls the pot, preserves my ~76bb stack for better spots, and lets me evaluate opponents’ intentions. Plan: check and call a small probe bet to see a turn; fold to heavy aggression; if I pick up a pair or a legitimate draw on the turn I’ll re‑evaluate and can become aggressive then.

I am on the button with Ace-Jack of spades in a three-way, 3-bet pot. The flop is Qs-5c-Kh. This board is very coordinated and likely hits the ranges of both Claude (who called a 3-bet) and Minni (who was the 3-bettor). Both opponents have checked to me. While I have a gutshot straight draw (to a Ten) and one overcard, I don't have enough current hand strength to bet for value against two opponents. Checking back allows me to realize my equity and potentially see a free turn card, which is the most prudent play in this specific multi-way dynamic.

I hit my set on the turn! 222 is now a very strong hand. Both opponents checked the flop showing some weakness or pot control with hands like AK, AQ, JJ, TT. Now I need to extract value. Betting around 55-60% pot is good - not too big to scare away medium strength hands, but building the pot with my monster. I'll bet 1600 to get value from Ax hands that might call thinking they have outs or from players who picked up equity.

I 3-bet preflop and checked the flop per plan to control the multiway pot. On the turn I only have Ace-high (A♥9♥) — with the 2♦ on the turn the backdoor heart flush is dead (only one card left), so my only clean improvements on the river are pairing my Ace or my 9. Raw outs = 3 Aces + 3 Nines = 6/46 ≈ 13% to pair; only the 3 Aces (~6.5%) will reliably beat obvious Kx/Qx value hands. Claude’s small but timely turn bet (1,600 into a 4,450 pot) looks like a probe/value attempt in a multiway pot and I need ~26.4% equity to justify a call (1,600 / (4,450+1,600)). My realistic equity versus Claude’s calling/calling range (Kx, Qx, pocket pairs, some broadways) is well below that threshold. Raising as a bluff is too risky here — Flash is still in the hand and a raise would commit a meaningful portion of my stack with virtually no fold equity against strong ranges. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot. Decision: fold.

I am holding Ace-Jack high with a gutshot straight draw (needing a Ten). The turn card is a deuce, which is largely a blank. Claude, who called a 3-bet pre-flop and checked the flop, has now led out for 1600 into a pot of approximately 2850. This 'donk' lead into the pre-flop 3-bettor (Minni) and the button caller (me) typically signifies a strong hand like a slow-played King, two pair, or a set, especially since Minni showed weakness by checking the flop. My pot odds are roughly 26% (1600 to call a total pot of 6050), but my direct equity is only about 8.7% (4 outs for the gutshot). Even if I consider my Ace as an out, I am likely behind and don't have the necessary equity or implied odds to justify a call, as a Ten on the river would be a very scary card that might kill my action. Therefore, the most disciplined play is to fold.
As Ts is a strong hand from UTG - suited broadway with an ace. Standard 2.5x open raise to 250. This hand plays well postflop with straight and flush potential.

I have 2♠3♠ — a small suited connector with decent post‑flop play and big implied odds. Calling 250 into a 400 pot is very cheap given my 7,600 stack (~3.3%), so I’ll see a flop rather than fold a hand that can make disguised straights/flushes. Claude opened UTG so his range is relatively strong; 3‑betting here would be over‑aggressive with a marginal hand and out of position versus callers behind. Note FiveTwo is short (1,000) and may shove — I’ll re‑evaluate if faced with a shove or a large 3‑bet (mostly fold to a sizable 3‑bet, call a small shove only with proper pot odds). Postflop plan: continue only with a pair, strong draw, or favourable board texture; otherwise control pot size and fold to heavy pressure.

I am holding King-Deuce suited in the Cutoff. UTG (Claude) opened to 250, and Middle Position (Minni) called. While my hand is suited, King-Deuce is very weak against an UTG opening range and a flat-caller. I would likely be dominated by better Kings or strong broadways. Given that I have the chip lead and there's a very short stack (FiveTwo) in the Big Blind who might jam, there is no reason to involve myself in this pot with a marginal hand. Folding is the disciplined play to preserve my stack.

My hand is 6s-3h offsuit, a complete trash hand with no high card value, straight potential, or suitedness. Claude raised from UTG, indicating strength, and Minni flat-called, suggesting a speculative hand but still building a pot. Facing a 2.5BB raise and a call, the pot is 650 with 250 to call, but postflop I'll be out of position against likely strong ranges from both. FiveTwo is short-stacked in BB but irrelevant here. Folding from the button is standard GTO play; no bluff equity or fold equity justifies entering with this. Preserve stack for better spots in the tournament.

I hold 7s 3c in the Small Blind, which is a very weak hand. Facing a raise from UTG and a call from Middle Position, I am out of position against ranges that dominate me. Additionally, the Big Blind is very short-stacked and could squeeze all-in. The pot odds do not justify calling with such poor equity and playability. Folding is the standard and correct play.

We’re getting an excellent price closing the action: call 150 to win a pot of 650 (and play for 800), so we need very little equity. QJo is dominated sometimes vs an UTG raise, but against a raise + flat ranges it still realizes enough equity, and folding is too tight given the pot odds. A squeeze-jam for ~10bb risks 900 to win 650 and will get called too often by big stacks, so the fold equity isn’t there. Take the cheap flop with an SPR < 1 and be ready to commit on favorable flops (top pair+, strong draws).

Multiway on a paired low flop (5-5-7), the UTG raiser has a big range advantage (overpairs, strong broadways) and either opponent can easily have a 5 or 7. With QJ we’re currently just two overcards/backdoors, and with SPR ~1 a lead/jam would get called too often by pairs and leave us in rough shape. Checking maximizes our chance to realize equity for free; if it checks through we can evaluate turns (especially Q/J/A/K) as potential shove cards, while versus significant action we can comfortably fold or make a very disciplined decision based on sizing.

Board is 5s 5h 7c - very dry paired board that likely missed both opponents. I have Ace high with a backdoor flush draw in spades. FiveTwo checked with only 850 behind. This is a good spot for a c-bet to take down the pot or get value from worse hands. Betting around 1/3 pot to keep it economical while still putting pressure.

Claude (UTG) opened and is c‑betting small (300 into a 1,100 pot). Calling 300 would commit me to a pot of 1,700, so I need roughly 300/1700 ≈ 17.6% equity to break even. With 2♠3♠ on 5♠5♥7♣ I have no pair and only very thin backdoor outs (pair by river ≈12.6%, backdoor flush/straight adds only a few percent). I’m out of position to the original raiser and have poor fold equity if I try to raise; a bluff/raise here is high variance and likely to be called or reraised by Claude’s relatively strong UTG range. FiveTwo’s short stack also creates the risk of a shove that forces a difficult decision. Given my preflop plan (continue only with a pair or a strong draw) and the pot‑odds math, this is a clear fold — preserve the stack for a better spot.

We’re extremely short (8.5bb) and facing a c-bet on a paired, low flop where Claude’s UTG range contains many overpairs, 7x, and occasionally trips. With QJ-high we have essentially just 6 overcard outs, and they’re not always clean (if he has AQ/KQ/overpairs, or any 5). Calling 300 would put in ~35% of our remaining stack and leave an SPR so tiny that we’re effectively committing without a real made hand or strong draw. Shoving doesn’t solve it either: Claude is getting great pot odds to call (needs ~22% equity vs our jam), so fold equity is too low and we’ll be called by pairs and even many overcards. Best play is to preserve the stack for a better shove/defend spot.

UTG with 3♥2♣ offsuit — essentially the worst playable hand. We're deep (≈73.5 BB) and in early position with five opponents yet to act; this hand has very poor equity, is easily dominated, and will be difficult to play post‑flop out of position. The pot currently only contains the blinds (150) and the min‑raise is 200, so neither limping nor raising is justified. Preserve stack and wait for a much stronger spot — fold.

I am holding Jack-Six offsuit (J6o) in Middle Position. This is a very weak hand that does not meet the requirements for an opening raise in a 6-max game. Additionally, FiveTwo is extremely short-stacked in the Small Blind (8 BBs), which increases the likelihood of a shove behind me. Claude, the other big stack, is in the Big Blind and has shown a willingness to play pots. There is no strategic value in entering the pot with this hand.

Preflop, unopened pot after UTG and MP folds, acting from cutoff with excellent position. T8o is a playable hand with straight potential and good postflop playability in position. Raising to steal blinds or isolate short-stacked SB (FiveTwo with ~8BB). Standard raise size of 2.5x BB (250) balances stealing and value, considering deep stacks behind but position advantage. Avoid limping as it plays out of position multiway poorly.

I am on the Button with [2h 5s], facing a raise to 250 from the Cutoff (Elon). This is a statistically very weak hand (trash) with little playability or equity. Standard strategy dictates folding such weak holdings against a raise, even in position. There are no particular reads or game dynamics suggesting a bluff or loose call is profitable here.

We’re very short (8bb) in the small blind facing a cutoff min-ish open. Flat-calling 200 more creates a ~600 pot with only ~550 behind (SPR < 1) while we’re out of position and invite the big blind to come along or squeeze, putting us in a nasty spot with a dominated king. The best way to realize our equity and maximize fold equity is to reshove now: we can win the 400 already in the middle immediately a large % of the time, and Kc6d has a useful blocker to strong Kx holdings plus reasonable equity when called given our stack depth. So we jam to leverage fold equity and avoid low-EV postflop decisions.

J6o is a weak hand facing a 3-bet. FiveTwo has only 50 chips behind after raising to 800, so they're essentially committed. I'm getting roughly 1.6:1 pot odds but J6o plays poorly against a 3-betting range. Easy fold.

Preflop, facing a large 3-bet shove-ish from short-stacked SB FiveTwo (only 50 chips left after his 800 bet). Pot is 1150, calling 550 more (total 800 from me) gives excellent pot odds of about 3:1, requiring only ~23% equity. T8o offsuit has decent equity (~28%) against a typical short-stack blind 3-bet range (JJ+, AK, AQs+, KQs, AJo+), plus straight potential and playability in position heads-up. His range may include bluffs or suited connectors given short stack dynamics and history of aggression. BB folded, button out, so no multiway. Postflop, he'll likely jam his remaining 50, turning it into a simple equity realization spot. Raising unnecessary as he commits anyway; calling maximizes EV without risking more chips unnecessarily. Folding too tight given odds and position.

We have only 50 chips left into a 1700 pot (SPR ~0.03). Any bet would be 50 and Elon is never folding getting absurd pot odds, so betting doesn’t accomplish anything as a bluff and doesn’t get value with K-high. Checking maximizes the chance he checks back this paired board and we can sometimes win at showdown with K-high; if he bets, we’ll be priced in anyway.

Flop board 7c-9h-7s pairs the sevens and misses much of my perceived range, but crucially, my Tc-8h connects perfectly for an open-ended straight draw (6x or Jx completes 6-7-8-9-10 or 7-8-9-10-J, 8 outs). Additional backdoor heart flush draw (8h + 9h) and minor backdoor clubs. Equity ~35-40% against FiveTwo's polarized SB 3-bet range (premiums like JJ+/AK or bluffs/suited connectors), which includes hands we dominate like Ax, 88-66, flush/straight draws, while behind sets/97/trips but with huge implied. He checks OOP with 50 chips left into 1700 pot (~34:1 odds if I check). Min-betting 50 all-in applies max pressure: gets called by worse (e.g., overcards, gutshots, weak pairs), fold equity from air/bluffs, denies equity improvement on turn. Checking invites a cheap bluff-jam or free card where he can hit; semibluff bet maximizes EV in position vs shortstack. Consistent with preflop aggressor/3-bet caller range including draws/straights.

We’re effectively committed: calling 50 to win a 1750 pot gives us absurd pot odds (need ~2.8% equity). On this paired 7-9-7 board, Elon’s tiny 50 stab can be extremely wide, and even K-high will realize well more than the required equity. Folding is a clear mistake; raising isn’t meaningful with only 50 behind.

I am in the UTG position with King-Four offsuit. In a 6-max game, King-Four offsuit is far too weak to open from early position, as it is easily dominated by the calling ranges of the five players behind me and plays poorly post-flop. Given the deep stacks of most players and the short stack of the Button, there is no reason to get involved with a marginal hand like this. I will fold.

Preflop, middle position, Flash UTG folded, facing blinds (pot 150, limp would be 100). KQs suited is a premium hand with good playability, high card strength, and suited connectivity. Standard to open raise for value and to build pot. Raise to 300 (3x BB) is appropriate sizing: builds pot without overcommitting, folds out worse hands, gets value from weaker broadways/calls from blinds. FiveTwo short on button might shove, but KQs has good equity vs shoving range. Deep stacks behind warrant controlled aggression. Avoid limping as it invites multiway pots with speculative hands.

I am in the Cutoff with 5h 7s, a very weak offsuit hand. Facing a raise to 300 from Elon in Middle Position, I am well behind his opening range. Additionally, there is a short stack on the Button and a deep stack in the Small Blind still to act, increasing the risk of a squeeze. Calling is -EV due to poor playability and reverse implied odds. Folding is the standard and correct play.

We’re 18bb on the button facing a 3x open from Elon. 64o is at the very bottom of the range: it plays poorly postflop, is frequently dominated when we make a pair, and doesn’t have the equity needed to justify a call. A bluff jam to 1800 would need very high fold equity, but Elon’s open size and the fact that the blinds can wake up and call make that too optimistic; when called we’re in very bad shape. Best EV and variance control here is to preserve the stack for a better reshove/steal spot.

6d8d is a suited connector but facing a raise from middle position while I'm out of position in the SB. With Minni still to act behind, calling puts me in a tough spot postflop. While suited connectors have playability, this is too marginal OOP. Folding is the disciplined play.

Elon opened to 300 from middle; I'm in the big blind with J♥3♦ — a raggy offsuit jack with very poor kicker and minimal post‑flop playability. Calling 200 requires ~31% equity vs his opening range, and out of position this hand will be dominated a lot of the time and vulnerable to continuation bets and turn aggression (Elon has been applying heavy pressure postflop). I don't want to play a marginal, easily‑dominated hand multi‑street OOP, nor 3‑bet bluff with such poor blockers. Fold and preserve the ~72BB stack for better spots where I have initiative or a hand with real playability.

89o is a speculative offsuit connector, playable in late position for implied odds but too weak from UTG in 6-handed play. Facing 5 opponents with varying stack sizes, including a short stack (FiveTwo at 18BB) who might shove, and aggressive players like Claude. Standard UTG range excludes this hand; folding preserves stack for stronger spots in tournament with 150 hands total.

I hold 5c 7h in Middle Position, which is a very weak starting hand (7-high offsuit). With the UTG player folding, I am next to act. This hand falls well outside of any reasonable opening range for this position in a 6-handed game. I have a healthy stack of 76.5 BBs and should preserve it for better opportunities. The correct play is to fold immediately.

We’re 6-handed in the cutoff with 18bb. T8o is a reasonable late-position open: we can pick up the blinds/antes-equivalent dead money and maintain initiative, and folding is a bit too tight given stack depth. Limping invites the button/blinds to realize equity cheaply and puts us in tougher spots postflop. With our shorter stack, I prefer a small open (min-raise) to risk less while still applying pressure; if we face a large 3-bet/shove from the big stacks, we can comfortably fold this hand.

2d7c is one of the weakest hands in poker. Even though I'm on the button with good position, this hand has virtually no playability. FiveTwo is short-stacked but that doesn't make calling with 72o profitable. Easy fold.

Fold. 4♣3♥ is too weak to defend the small blind out of position against a CO open (to 200) with the big blind yet to act. I’d have to call 150 out of position with poor equity and limited implied odds (the opener only has 1.6k behind), and a 3-bet or shove is out of line with this holding. Preserving a 72BB stack and waiting for a better, playable spot is the +EV line here.

I am in the Big Blind with Q7o. FiveTwo, who is currently the short stack with only 1,600 chips (16 BB), has raised to 200 from the Cutoff. I have already posted 100 as the Big Blind, so it costs me 100 more to call. This gives me pot odds of 3.5:1. Given the excellent price and my dominant chip lead, I will call to see a flop. I must play cautiously post-flop, as FiveTwo is likely looking for a double-up and may commit the rest of their stack on many textures.

I have flopped middle pair (sevens) on a monotone board of all diamonds. While I have a pair, the board is very dangerous as any diamond makes a flush and any King beats me. FiveTwo is extremely short-stacked (16 BBs) and was the pre-flop aggressor. I will check to the raiser to see their continuation bet. Given their stack size, they may commit the rest of their chips here with a wide range, including Kx, high diamonds, or even air. I'll check and re-evaluate based on their action.

Monotone K-high flop heavily favors hands containing a diamond. With 8cTd and no diamond, we have essentially no immediate equity and very poor ability to continue versus a check-raise or even a check-call line. Given our ~16bb stack, preserving chips and avoiding getting blown off our hand is higher EV than firing a low-equity c-bet into a big stack who can continue with any diamond, Kx, and some pairs. We’ll check back, keep the pot manageable, and re-evaluate turns (possibly bluffing later if the runout shifts and Flash remains passive).

I have a pair of 7s on a monotone King-high board. FiveTwo checked back on the flop, which suggests they might not have a King or a completed flush, though they could be holding a high diamond. Since I have some showdown value but don't want to bloat the pot against a short stack who might shove over a lead, I will check again and see the river. This keeps the pot small and allows me to potentially catch a bluff or see a cheap showdown.

Turn improves us to a pair (8x) after we checked back the monotone flop. Flash checking again caps him a bit toward A-high/medium pairs/one-diamond hands that didn’t want to bet, plus some slowplayed Kx. We can now bet for thin value/protection: deny equity to hands like Ax/Qx with a diamond, get called by worse pairs (7x/3x/weak 8x), and sometimes take it down immediately. With ~16bb we also want to keep the sizing small so we’re not pot-committed and can comfortably fold to a check-raise jam (which will be heavily weighted to strong Kx/flushes/strong draws).

FiveTwo checked back on the monotone flop, which typically indicates they don't have a made flush or a strong King. Their turn bet of 250 into 450 after the 8h arrived looks like a delayed continuation bet or a stab at the pot, potentially with a single high diamond or a weak pair. I have middle pair (sevens), and with pot odds of 2.8:1, I have enough equity to justify a call against a short-stacked opponent who may be bluffing or betting a worse hand. I will call to see the river and re-evaluate, keeping in mind that they only have 1,100 chips behind.

On the river, the board has paired with a second Trey. My current hand is two pair, Sevens and Treys with a Queen kicker. Looking back at the action, FiveTwo checked the monotone flop, which makes it less likely they have a flush or a strong King. Their turn bet of 250 seemed like a delayed lead or a semi-bluff. Since the river 3s is relatively neutral unless they were holding exactly a Trey, I will check to see if FiveTwo wants to check behind for a showdown or attempt another bluff. Betting myself likely only gets called by hands that beat me (Kings, Flushes, or Treys), so checking is the most prudent way to realize my showdown value.

River pairs the 3, giving us two pair (8s and 3s) with a K kicker. After BB checks flop, check-calls a small turn stab, then checks river, his range is often capped toward one-pair hands (7x, some pocket pairs, occasional 8x chops) and some give-ups; strong hands (flushes/Kx/full houses) exist but are less frequent given the passive line and will often either call or spring a check-raise. With our short stack, we want thin value without pot-committing ourselves, so I’ll bet small and comfortably fold if he check-raises/jams (that line is heavily weighted to Kx/flush/full house).

FiveTwo's river bet of 300 into a pot of 950 provides me with pot odds of approximately 4:1. My hand, which is two pair (Sevens and Treys) with a Queen kicker, is likely ahead of bluffs, missed straight draws, or smaller pocket pairs. FiveTwo's decision to check the monotone flop makes it less probable that they hold a made flush or a high King. Given the very favorable price and the possibility that the opponent is betting a weaker hand for value or bluffing their remaining short stack, I have more than enough equity to justify a call.

I am in UTG position with 4h 2h, a very weak suited one-gapper. Acting first pre-flop with five opponents left to act puts me at a significant disadvantage. The hand has very low playability and equity, especially out of position. With a comfortable stack of 76.5 BB, there is no need to take unnecessary risks with such a marginal hand. The standard and most profitable play is to fold.

KQo is a strong, high-card broadway hand in 6-max and performs well as an open from middle position. Limping invites multiway play against deeper stacks and reduces our fold equity; folding would be too tight. With ~26bb, we want a raise size that pressures blinds and denies equity without bloating the pot unnecessarily versus big stacks behind. A standard ~2.5bb open accomplishes this and keeps our range credible for future streets.

Ts8h is a marginal hand facing a raise from middle position. While I have position on FiveTwo, this hand doesn't play well enough to call - it's not suited, not connected enough, and there are still players behind me. FiveTwo is also short-stacked (2350) which means if I call and they shove post-flop, I'm often in a tough spot. Clean fold here.

I’m on the button with pocket 7s (7d7h) facing FiveTwo’s open to 250. I have a 72bb stack and FiveTwo is relatively short (~23bb) and opens frequently, so my priority is to isolate and take the initiative rather than limp/call and allow the blinds to create a multiway pot where a medium pair loses equity. I’ll 3-bet to 900 — large enough to put real pressure on the opener and discourage loose calls from the blinds, but not so large that a short-stack shove becomes an unplayable decision. If FiveTwo shoves I can call comfortably (we have good equity vs a wide shoving range); if called, I’ll use position to c-bet dry boards and pot-control or fold on heavy resistance when I miss. Raise to 900.

I am holding 8d-6c in the Small Blind. There has been an open-raise to 250 from FiveTwo and a 3-bet to 900 from Minni. My hand is very weak and does not play well post-flop, especially out of position in a 3-bet pot. There is no reason to continue with this hand against two ranges that likely dominate mine.

Facing a 3-bet from Minni on the button after FiveTwo's open from middle position. My 8s6s is a suited connector with good playability post-flop, straight and flush potential. Pot odds are favorable: calling 800 more into 1300 (about 2.6:1), and the pot could grow if FiveTwo calls or shoves. FiveTwo has a short stack of 2350, so he might fold or commit, but my hand performs well multiway or heads-up against a button 3-betting range which is likely wide. Raising is unnecessary risk with a speculative hand; folding is too tight given the price and implied odds with deep effective stacks (Minni 6300, me 7175). Calling keeps the pot multiway-friendly and realizes equity.

We’re in a nasty spot if we just flat: KQo in a 3-bet pot that’s already gone multiway (BTN 3-bet + BB cold call) creates a very low SPR situation where we’ll miss a lot and be forced into committing decisions out of position versus the Button’s range (which dominates KQ with AK/AQ/KQ often). Folding is defensible, but the pot is already huge relative to our stack: there’s ~2100 in dead money and we can shove ~26bb with strong blockers (K and Q remove combos of KK/QQ/AK/KQ). A 4-bet non-all-in leaves an awkward sub-1 SPR anyway and gives both players good odds; the clean highest-EV line is to apply maximum pressure and realize our fold equity. When called, we still retain workable equity versus typical calling ranges (pairs and big aces).

I’m on the button with pocket 7s after 3-betting to 900. FiveTwo has shoved / 4-bet to 2,600 total. The pot is 4,450 and it costs me 1,700 more to call (I’d be 2,600 invested total). That gives me roughly 27.6% pot odds (1,700 / (4,450+1,700)). Versus a short-stack shoving range (wide — pairs, broadways, suited connectors, etc.) pocket 7s have well above ~28% equity (roughly in the 40–50% ballpark depending on exactly how wide they shove), so a call is +EV. Raising to 4,300 would overcommit into a multiway/side-pot (Elon already has chips in) and folding here is too tight given the pot odds and my equity. I’ll call the all-in for 1,700 more (2,600 total) and play the board in position; if Elon chips in for a side pot I’ll proceed cautiously in that side pot and use position to control the pot when I miss.

Already invested 900 calling the button 3-bet with 8s6s, a suited connector with excellent playability, straight/flush potential, and good multiway equity. FiveTwo's 4-bet shove from middle position after opening is polarized: strong value (QQ+, AK) or bluffs, but given short stack history, likely weighted toward value. Minni's call of the 4-bet from her wide 3-bet range suggests strength but not nuts, possibly pairs, broadways. Pot 6150, calling 1700 more (total 2600 committed) offers ~4.6:1 odds (need ~18% equity), and 86s realizes 25-35% vs typical ranges plus implied odds from Minni's 4600 effective stack. Raising commits too much preflop with speculative hand; folding loses too much after prior investment and ignores pot odds/postflop potential vs shorter stacks. Call to realize equity multiway now heads-up effectively postflop.

Preflop I called a large 4-bet with 8s6s as a speculative suited connector in the BB with favorable implied odds multiway against wide ranges and deep stacks. However, the flop Qd2dQc completely misses my hand: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot at best with runner-runner), only backdoor spade flush and straight potential. FiveTwo's short-stack 4-bet shove after opening is polarized but favors value (QQ+, sets, AK); Minni's button 3-bet and call behind suggests medium strength like QQ, KQ, AJ suited, or draws/bluffs that hit pairs. Pot is 7850 with 4675 behind (effective vs Minni's 4600). As first to act OOP with zero equity (~10-20% at best vs calling range), checking is mandatory. No reason to bet/raise into likely strong range; will fold to any bet from Minni to preserve stack for better spots. Goal is long-term stack maximization over 150 hands.

Pot is 7,850 and FiveTwo is already all-in — I can’t win his chips by betting now. I have pocket 7s on Qd 2d Qc (paired queens) which is an underpair to the board and behind any Queen or overpair. Betting OOP here would bloat the side pot vs Elon and will mostly get called by hands that beat me (Qx, bigger pairs) while folding only the weakest holdings — I have very little fold equity. I’ll check to control the pot, see what Elon does, and preserve stack size. If Elon bets a small amount I can call to realize equity; if he fires large I can fold and keep chips for better spots; if checked through I’ll re-evaluate the turn and only become aggressive if I improve (set) or pick up a strong read.

On the turn with 8s6s facing a Qd2dQc4s board, I have complete air: no pair, no straight draw (gutshot requires 5-7, unlikely), only runner-runner backdoor spade flush or straight possibilities. Preflop I called a large multiway 4-bet pot with a speculative suited connector expecting multiway playability and implied odds, but the flop paired queens and missed entirely, and turn brings a blank 4s that doesn't help. Pot is 7850 with 4675 behind (effective ~4600 vs Minni). As first to act OOP, betting/raising is -EV: no equity to realize, bluffing into a button who checked flop (possibly medium strength like QQ/KQ/AJ/draws) risks her calling with better or raising. Checking preserves stack; I will fold to any bet from Minni, as pot odds won't justify calling without equity. Long-term stack maximization requires folding marginal spots over 150 hands.

I have 7♢7♡ on Q♦ 2♦ Q♣ 4♠. FiveTwo is already all‑in so any bet from me only affects Elon in the side pot. I’m an underpair to the board and behind any Q or any larger pair; betting here has very little fold equity and will mostly bloat the side pot and get called by hands that beat me. Pot control is the priority — preserve chips and use position to react. I’ll check and see what Elon does: call small bets to realize equity, fold to large value bets, and re-evaluate the river (only press if I improve or get a very specific read).

River with 8s6s on Qd2dQc4sTh board: complete air, no pair, no draws completed (spade flush draw missed, no straight outs). Preflop speculative call was for playability/implied odds, but board paired queens, favoring Minni's 3-bet/4-bet call range (QQ+, KQ, AQ, AJs, pocket pairs, some bluffs/draws). She checked flop/turn, possibly medium strength (one pair, trapped trips) or weak. OOP first to act with zero showdown value vs her range (~0-5% equity), pot 7850, stack 4675. Betting/raising is -EV bluff: commits nearly all stack into calling range (any Qx, 22+, broadways), low fold equity vs button who checks weak hands behind but calls/raises value. Checking maximizes EV: induces bluffs/checks behind, preserves stack for 90+ hands remaining by avoiding spew. Fold if she bets (pot odds insufficient without equity). Long-term stack growth prioritizes discipline.

Board: Q♦ 2♦ Q♣ 4♠ T♥. I hold 7♦7♥ and FiveTwo is already all‑in, so any bet only affects Elon in the side pot. I have an underpair and am behind any Q or any pair above 7; there is virtually no fold equity on the river and betting would just bloat the side pot and get called by hands that beat me. With pot control the priority (pot = 7,850, my stack = 4,600) I’ll preserve chips and check to see if Elon shows aggression or checks behind. I’ll call small, non‑committal bets to realize equity but fold to large value bets unless I pick up a strong read that he’s bluffing.

UTG 6-handed with 4-2 offsuit is well below our opening range and plays terribly postflop (dominated, poor equity realization). With no incentive to defend/limp UTG and plenty of better spots, we preserve stack and avoid getting isolated by position behind.
97o in middle position is a clear fold. This hand is too weak to open from MP with 4 players behind me. I have a healthy stack at 171 BB - no need to get involved with marginal holdings.

7♣4♦ offsuit is a clear fold from the cutoff. It’s far below any reasonable open-raising range and plays very poorly multiway or out of position. The button and small blind are deep and capable defenders (Elon especially shows aggression and frequent defenses), so attempting a steal risks a 3-bet or getting called and playing big pots with a dominated hand. With a healthy 46 BB stack there’s no need to gamble — preserve chips and wait for a better spot or a hand with real playability. Folding is the optimal EV play here.

I am on the button with 5-6 suited, which is a premium speculative hand in this position. It has folded to me, and the blinds are relatively short-stacked compared to my chip lead (Elon with ~46BB and Pro with ~75BB). Raising here is standard to put pressure on the blinds and take control of the pot with a hand that has great post-flop playability. A 2.5x open to 250 is consistent with previous action at the table.

Facing a button raise after three folds, Flash's range is very wide including many broadway hands, suited connectors, and Ax. QJo has excellent equity against this range (dominates hands like KJo, QTo, JTo, and blocks QQ/JJ), good playability with straight potential, and solid blockers. From the SB, out of position, a 3-bet isolates against the BB and applies pressure preflop. With 46BB effective stack, sizing to 800 (3.2x the open) is standard for value/light 3-bet, building a nice pot if called while folding out worse. History shows Flash aggressive but folds to pressure sometimes (e.g., Hand 13). Folding or flatting OOP with this hand is suboptimal; 3-bet maximizes EV.

I am in the Big Blind with [Qd Kd] (King-Queen suited). The action involves a Button open to 250 by Flash and a 3-bet to 800 by Elon from the Small Blind. While KQs is a strong hand, facing a cold 3-bet is a dangerous spot. Elon's 3-bet size (8bb) represents a significant portion of his 39bb stack, indicating a strong range or a committed bluff. Additionally, the original raiser (Flash) is still to act behind me with a massive stack, and calling invites a potential 4-bet squeeze. KQs is often dominated by 3-betting ranges (AK, AQ, KK, QQ) and plays poorly against an all-in calling range. Given my stack depth (75bb), I should avoid high-variance spots where I am likely dominated or flipping at best. The disciplined play is to fold.

I am on the button with 5c-6c and opened for 250. Elon, in the small blind, has 3-bet to 800. I am getting approximately 2.1:1 on a call (550 to win a total pot of 1700). While Elon's stack is relatively short (around 38BB), I have a massive chip lead and am in position. Suited connectors like 56s have enough equity against a typical 3-bet range (roughly 34-35%) to justify a call, and playing in position allows me to realize that equity better or potentially take the pot away if he checks the flop. Folding is too tight given the price and position, and 4-betting is unnecessary given our stack depths.

Preflop 3-bet with QJo was correct as planned, isolating vs wide BTN range and building pot OOP. Flop Jc Kh 7d hits me hard with top pair (Jacks) + Q kicker + gutshot straight draw blocker (Qs blocks QTs). Board is somewhat coordinated (possible Kx, QTs, flush draw w/ diamonds, str8 draws), but my range hits this well (AJs, KJs, QJs, sets, etc.). Flash's 3-bet calling range from BTN: KK+, AKo/s, KQ, maybe AJs/KJs, broadway. I have ~35-45% equity vs that (beats AJ/KQ, behind AK/KK). OOP first to act, pot 1700, stacks ~38BB eff for me. C-bet for thin value/protection vs weaker Kx/AJ/draws, deny equity to floats/str8 draws. Denies BB (but folded). Size 2/3 pot (1100) balances value/bluff (mix in some air sometimes). Check risks giving free card to draws or inducing bluffs but pot controls weaker hands; betting maximizes EV long-term. Flash aggressive postflop but folds to c-bets sometimes (e.g., Hand 52). Continue rep strong polarized 3-bet range.

On this board of Jack-King-Seven, my 5-6 of clubs has missed completely. I have no pair and no real draws other than a very weak backdoor flush. Elon 3-bet pre-flop and is now leading for a large sizing (1100 into 1700) relative to his remaining stack. Given that Elon only has 1675 chips left behind after this bet, he is likely committed to the pot with any reasonable part of his 3-betting range (AK, KQ, KJ, or even just high pocket pairs). There is no fold equity to be had by bluffing, and I have almost zero showdown value or pot equity. Folding is the correct play to preserve my stack.

K6o from UTG is too weak to open. It's easily dominated by better kings and plays poorly postflop from early position. With 90 hands left in the tournament and a healthy stack, no need to take marginal spots like this.

Ace-Queen offsuit (A♠ Q♦) in middle position is a clear open-raise hand — strong enough for initiative but not a hand I want to play passively multiway. UTG folded, pot is small (150) and calling 100 would allow multiple players behind to see a cheap flop; I prefer to isolate and build the pot with a standard 3x BB open. A raise to 300 is a normal, table-consistent sizing that gives fold equity against the blinds/button and keeps SPR reasonable with my 46 BB stack. If I face a single reasonably-sized 3-bet I’ll continue or call depending on size and player; I’ll re-evaluate heavily if confronted with a large 3-bet/shove. Raise to 300 to take the initiative and protect my equity.

I am holding 4h4s in the Cutoff. A pocket pair is generally worth a call to set-mine, especially given that Minni's stack is relatively short (4300 total) and I have a significant chip lead over the table. The 300 bet is standard for Minni. While I could raise, calling allows me to see a flop for a relatively cheap price and try to hit a set, which would likely stack Minni if she has a premium hand. I'll call and evaluate on the flop.

T4o is a speculative trash hand with poor playability, low equity multiway, and no suited or connected value. Facing a raise from Minni (who has a shorter effective stack of 4300) and a call from Flash (deep), entering a bloated pot OOP or multiway is -EV. Button position doesn't justify calling with this hand; better to preserve stack for premium spots in a tournament with 90 hands left. Standard fold to avoid difficult postflop decisions on dry boards or dominated ranges.

I am in the Small Blind with 7d Jc, facing a raise to 300 from Minni and a call from Flash. My hand is very weak (J7 offsuit) and performs poorly multi-way, especially out of position. Although I am getting decent pot odds (calling 250 to win a pot of 750), the reverse implied odds and lack of playability make this a clear fold. I do not want to play a bloated pot from the worst position with a trash hand.

We’re getting a very good price in the big blind (200 to win 750 now, and we’ll see a flop multiway). K2s is weak and can be dominated when we make a king, but the suitedness gives us enough equity/implied odds (flushes, occasional 2-pair) to defend rather than fold, and it’s not strong enough to justify a squeeze/3-bet out of position versus an opener and a caller.

We flop two pair (K2) on Kc-7s-2s in a 3-way pot. This is a strong value hand but it’s vulnerable: many Kx will continue, and there are plenty of spade/straight backdoor combos that have meaningful equity. Checking risks it going check-through multiway, giving free cards to flush draws and allowing awkward turn action. Leading also keeps ranges wide (gets calls from Kx, 7x, 2x, spade draws) while charging the draws. A medium sizing accomplishes value + protection without forcing only very strong hands to continue.

I opened to 300 preflop with A♦Q♦ to take the initiative. On K♣ 7♠ 2♠ I hold A♠Q♦ — two overcards plus a backdoor (double-runner) spade possibility and a strong blocker to some bluff/continuation combos. FiveTwo’s 650 into a 1600 pot gives me roughly 28–30% pot odds to call, and my hand’s equity versus a wide continuing range (Kx, medium pairs, draws, some bluffs) is in that neighborhood — calling is +EV here. Raising to the min 1300 would bloat the pot and commit a large chunk of my 4300 stack with only overcards; it also risks getting reraised by value (Kx/sets) or isolating against worse/unknown action from Flash behind. I’ll call 650 to realize equity, keep the pot controllable, and reevaluate on the turn (push for value or fold to heavy action depending on the card and opponents’ behavior).

On a flop of 2s 7s Kc, the Big Blind (FiveTwo) has led out for 650 into a pot of 950, and the pre-flop raiser (Minni) has called. My pocket fours are likely behind given the strength shown by both opponents on a board containing a King and a flush draw. I have no significant draws and only two outs to improve to a set, which would still be vulnerable. Given the multi-way action and the sizing of the bet, I will fold and preserve my stack.

Turn pairs the king giving us KKK22 (kings full of deuces) — a very strong full house. Only realistic better hands are KK (quads) and K7 (kings full of sevens), which are a small part of Minni’s preflop open/call-flop range. Most of her continues are Kx (now trips/full house on river), plus some 7x/spade-draw type hands that may still pay once. With Minni short (3650 effective) and pot 2250, we should bet for clear value and start building a pot that allows an easy river shove without forcing folds from worse Kx. A ~2/3 pot sizing targets KQ/KJ/KT and keeps weaker holdings from instantly giving up while setting up a small river jam.

Turn: I have A♠Q♦ and the turn K♠ completes my nut spade flush. However the board is paired (Kc/Ks), which enables full houses/quads (pocket 7s/2s, K+2/7 combos, pocket Ks) that beat my flush. FiveTwo’s line (call preflop, raise flop to 650, raise turn to 1500) is value-heavy and can include both boats and made/semibluffing flushes. Raising to the min (3000) would bloat the pot and effectively commit a large portion of my 3650 stack against a range that contains dangerous full-house combinations — I don’t want to get committed facing a shove. Calling 1500 keeps the pot under control, realizes my equity with the nut flush versus weaker/semibluff hands, and leaves ~2150 behind to re-evaluate the river and extract value or fold to clear full-house action.

We river kings full (KKK22) and are essentially at the top of our range. The only realistic hands that beat us are KK (quads) and K7 (kings full of sevens), both very unlikely given preflop (KK mostly 3-bets) and the line. Minni’s turn call heavily weights her toward Kx (now trips), and also includes flushes (3 spades on board) and some stubborn two-pair (K7/77/2x) that can’t fold for a small river bet. With 5250 in the pot and Minni only 2150 behind, the highest-EV play is to put her all-in: it’s a ~41% pot jam that gets paid by a wide value region and denies any chance of a check-back.

I hold A♠ Q♦ on a board Kc Ks 2s 7s 5h. I do not have a made two‑pair or better — my best five is the pair of kings with A/Q kickers — so I’m only beating pure bluffs and very thin value. FiveTwo’s line (calling preflop, raising the flop to 650, raising the turn to 1500 after the board pairs, and now betting 2150 on the river) is extremely value‑heavy and consistent with sets/full houses/quads (pocket 2s/7s that became full, Kx that turned trips/full, K+small to make a river/full house combos, or rarely quads). To call 2150 into a 7400 pot I need ~22.5% equity; my hand has far less equity versus that heavy value range. There are too few believable bluff combinations given this line to justify risking my last chips. Preserve the stack and fold.

UTG with K♣7♦ — a weak, easily dominated hand that plays poorly out of position. Blinds are 50/100, my stack is 2,150 (≈21.5bb). The pot is 150 and the minimum raise is to 200. Opening or limp-calling from UTG with K7o is a negative-EV line: it invites 3-bets from deeper stacks (Claude/Flash/Pro/FiveTwo) and puts me in difficult multiway spots out of position. With 21bb I should pick better spots to accumulate chips. Fold and wait for a stronger hand or better position.

I am in Middle position with 7c4c, a speculative suited hand. While it has potential to flop big, current table dynamics show significant pre-flop aggression from players like Claude, Elon, and FiveTwo. Minni has already folded, and I am first to act after the fold. Raising with such a marginal hand from this position is high risk, and calling is generally suboptimal as it invites further raises from behind. Given my healthy stack of 16,950, there is no need to play weak hands out of position. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Preflop in cutoff with no action ahead (UTG and MP folded, blinds posted, pot 150, limp would cost 100). Tc3h is a trash hand: offsuit, poor connectivity, dominated by any ten or three opponent might have. Post-flop playability is awful - few draws, low equity vs calling ranges. Even in steal spot from CO, T3o is outside standard opening ranges (typically 25-35% hands like suited connectors, broadways, pairs). With 55BB effective stack and deeper opponents behind (Pro BTN 75BB, blinds deep), folding preserves stack for premium spots. History shows aggressive opens but not with speculative junk like this. Goal is long-term stack growth over 150 hands, not spew chips.

I am on the Button with King-Six offsuit (K6o). The action has folded to me. While the Button is a strong position to steal the blinds from, K6o is a marginal hand with poor playability post-flop due to the weak kicker and lack of suit connectivity. Furthermore, the Big Blind (Claude) is the chip leader and has shown a tendency to defend his blind and play aggressively post-flop (e.g., calling down bets in Hands #11 and #39). Initiating a pot against a loose-aggressive big stack with a weak hand is likely -EV. Therefore, the disciplined play is to fold and wait for a better spot.
